title: Real Estate Client Care Specialist (Seller Support)
description:
Why Join Us?

- Career Growth: Opportunities to step into leadership roles as our Client Care and Listing departments expand.

- Performance Bonuses

- Competitive Perks: Including 13th-month pay.

- Work-Life Balance: Paid leave to help you stay refreshed and focused.

- Long-Term Stability: A dedicated role within a high-volume, growing US real estate team.

- Supportive Environment: Work closely with listing agents who value your role in getting homes sold.



What We’re Looking For

We need a proactive communicator who understands that selling a home can be stressful. Your job is to make the process smooth and easy for our clients.

- Seller-Centric Mindset: Empathetic and able to build trust with homeowners throughout the selling process.

- Future Leader Qualities: Someone who takes ownership of the “Listing Journey” and looks for ways to improve client experience.

- Proactive Communication: Provide updates before the seller even asks.

- Reliability: Punctual and dependable, available to work Eastern Standard Time (EST) hours.

- Flexibility: Open to working weekends when needed.



Responsibilities

- Seller Concierge: Act as the main point of contact for homeowners after they sign with us, guiding them from listing to closing.

- Listing Agent Support: Assist agents with administrative tasks, document collection, and preparation to get homes live on the market.

- Client Nurture: Maintain consistent follow-ups via phone, text, and
 so sellers feel supported and informed.

- Appointment Coordination: Schedule photography, signage, and inspections, ensuring vendors and sellers are aligned.

- CRM & Record Keeping: Keep accurate records of all seller interactions and listing milestones in Follow Up Boss (FUB).

- Document Compliance: Ensure all listing agreements and disclosures are properly filed and signed to keep the process moving toward a sale.



Important Requirement

Applicants must provide a contact reference from a previous direct supervisor, a recommendation letter, or a Certificate of Employment (COE) before being scheduled for an interview.



How to Apply

 
 with the following:

- Updated resume with references from previous employers

- PDF DISC result from 
/free-disc-test

- A short video introducing yourself, explaining why you want to join The Tricoli Team, and how you provide excellent service to home sellers

- Your onlinejobs.ph profile link



Subject Line: Real Estate Client Care Specialist

title: Executive / Personal Assistant to the Founder & CEO — STEAMUP Academy
description:
?? READ THIS ENTIRE POST BEFORE APPLYING. Applications that do not follow the instructions at the bottom will NOT be considered.



About STEAMUP Academy

STEAMUP Academy is a mission-driven educational organization dedicated to inspiring the next generation of thinkers, innovators, and leaders through hands-on programs in Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Mathematics (STEAM). We are growing fast and need a sharp, dependable right-hand person to support our Founder & CEO directly.



About This Role

This is not a basic data-entry VA role. We need someone who can think, act, and communicate at the level of a high-performing executive assistant. You will work side-by-side with the Founder/CEO to manage their time, communications, and daily priorities — so they can focus on leading and growing the organization.

If you are proactive, solutions-oriented, and take pride in excellent English communication, this role is for you.



Key Responsibilities

Executive & Administrative Support

Manage and organize the CEO's
 inbox — draft, respond, and flag priority messages

Maintain and manage the CEO's calendar, including scheduling meetings, calls, and appointments

Prepare meeting agendas, take detailed notes, and follow up on action items

Handle correspondence on behalf of the CEO 
s, letters, messages)

Research topics, vendors, partners, and opportunities as directed

Create reports, presentations, and summaries for leadership and stakeholders

Manage travel logistics and itineraries when needed



Operations & Project Coordination

Track ongoing projects and ensure deadlines are met

Coordinate between tea
mbers, contractors, and external partners

Maintain organized digital filing systems (Google Drive, cloud folders)

Assist with onboarding new hires and contractors

Support grant research, documentation, and reporting workflows

Monitor and order supplies; manage vendor relations as needed



Communications & Relationship Management



Draft and proofread professional communications and documents

Act as a liaison between the CEO and internal/external stakeholders

Help manage community partnerships, client inquiries, and follow-up communications

Assist with preparing content or talking points for public-facing activities



Finance & Administrative Support

Assist with basic bookkeeping and budget tracking (QuickBooks or similar)

Maintain databases and records for financial information and personnel

Support the preparation of financial reports and proposals

Track invoices, receipts, and reimbursements



What We're Looking For 

Required:

Exceptional written and spoken English — this is non-negotiable. You will be communicating professionally with US-based clients, partners, and stakeholders

At least 2 years of experience as a VA, executive assistant, or operations role

Strong independent thinking. You should be able to take a task and run with it, not wait to be told every step

Excellent problem-solving skills. If something comes up, you find a solution and report back

Proficiency in Google Workspace (Gmail, Docs, Sheets, Calendar, Drive)

Experience with Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook)

High level of discretion. You will have access to sensitive business information

Reliable internet connection, laptop/computer, and a quiet work environment

Ability to work US Eastern Time hours (or significant overlap)



Preferred / Big Plus:

Experience supporting a founder, CEO, or executive in the US

Familiarity with project management tools (Asana, Trello, Notion, ClickUp)

Basic bookkeeping or QuickBooks experience

Background or interest in education, nonprofits, or youth programs

Experience with Canva for simple document formatting





You Are a Great Fit If You...

Take ownership. You don't wait to be micromanaged.

Communicate clearly and proactively — you give updates before being asked.

Are calm under pressure and can juggle multiple priorities.

Notice problems before they become crises and solve them quietly.

Are loyal, trustworthy, and treat confidential information with the utmost care.

Love learning and growing in a dynamic, mission-driven environment.



What We Offer

Competitive pay: $4.00 – $6.00 USD/hour (based on experience)

Full-time, stable, long-term position (we value loyalty and retention)

Opportunity to grow with a fast-growing educational organization

Supportive, mission-driven team culture

Professional development opportunities



How to Apply

To be considered, your application MUST include all of the following:

Start your message with the phrase: "STEAM Powers the Future" (this confirms you read the full post)

A brief introduction video about yourself in your own words, no templates

Your most relevant work experience and how it applies to this role

One example of a time you solved a problem independently without being directed by your manager

Your availability and time zone

Your resume or CV (PDF preferred)



Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be reviewed. We look forward to meeting you!

title: Chief Operating Officer
description:
Chief Operating Officer (COO) & Automation Systems Strategist

Position Overview

We are seeking a highly skilled, execution-driven Chief Operating Officer (COO) who can lead the operational infrastructure of a fast-growing nurse entrepreneurship company. This individual will be responsible for overseeing day-to-day operations while architecting, optimizing, and managing the automation systems that drive scalability, revenue, and client experience.

This role requires someone who is both a strategic thinker and a systems builder—someone who can see the big picture while ensuring every automation, funnel, and workflow executes flawlessly.

 

Core Responsibilities

1. Operational Leadership

• Oversee daily business operations to ensure efficiency, scalability, and profitability

• Translate CEO vision into executable plans and systems

• Identify operational gaps and implement solutions quickly

• Manage internal workflows, team accountability, and project execution

• Ensure all departments (sales, marketing, client delivery) are aligned and functioning smoothly

 

2. Systems & Automation Management (PRIMARY FOCUS)

GoHighLevel (GHL)

• Build and manage CRM pipelines, workflows, and automation sequences

• Create and optimize funnels, landing pages, and campaigns

• Implement lead tracking, follow-up systems, and nurture sequences

• Troubleshoot automation issues and ensure system reliability

ThriveCart

• Manage checkout pages, payment plans, subscriptions, and upsells

• Handle payment failures, dunning processes, and customer billing issues

• Optimize checkout flows to increase conversions and revenue

Zapier

• Build and maintain integrations between platforms

• Automate workflows across CRM,
, payment systems, and course platforms

• Ensure seamless data flow and eliminate manual processes

Automation Strategy

• Design end-to-end automated systems for lead generation, sales, onboarding, and retention

• Continuously optimize workflows to increase efficiency and reduce human error

• Document all systems and processes for scalability

 

3. Revenue & Client Experience Optimization

• Ensure seamless client journey from lead ? sale ? onboarding ? delivery

• Monitor and improve conversion rates across funnels and campaigns

• Implement automation that enhances client communication and retention

• Track key metrics and optimize systems for increased revenue

 

4. Tech & Infrastructure Oversight

• Own the full tech stack and ensure all platforms are integrated and functioning

• Troubleshoot system issues quickly and proactively

• Recommend new tools or systems that improve efficiency and scale

 

5. Team & Project Management

• Lead and manage tea
mbers responsible for execution (VAs, tech, marketing, etc.)

• Assign tasks, track deliverables, and ensure deadlines are met

• Create SOPs and training systems for team scalability

 

Required Qualifications

• Proven experience as a COO, Operations Director, or Systems Strategist in an online business

• Advanced expertise in:

o GoHighLevel (GHL)

o ThriveCart

o Zapier

• Strong understanding of funnels, automations, and digital sales systems

• Ability to think strategically while executing at a high level

• Experience managing teams and scaling operations

• Strong problem-solving and decision-making skills

 

Key Traits for Success

• Systems thinker who sees inefficiencies before they become problems

• Highly accountable and results-driven

• Tech-savvy with a deep understanding of automation ecosystems

• Calm under pressure and able to manage multiple moving parts

• Committed to excellence and continuous improvement

 

Success Metrics (What Winning Looks Like)

• Fully automated and optimized client journey

• Increased conversion rates across funnels and checkouts

• Reduced manual workload through automation

• Seamless system integrations with minimal errors

• Team operating efficiently with clear systems and accountability

title: Social Media & Marketing Manager
description:
Job Type: Full-Time | Remote

Schedule: Monday – Friday | Hours aligned with US Eastern Time

Compensation: $3.00 – $5.00 USD/hour (based on experience)

Start Date: ASAP



?? READ THIS ENTIRE POST BEFORE APPLYING. Applications that do not follow the instructions at the bottom will NOT be considered.



About STEAMUP Academy

STEAMUP Academy is a fast-growing, mission-driven educational organization that inspires and empowers young thinkers, innovators, and leaders through hands-on programs in Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Mathematics (STEAM). Our brand is bold, colorful, and creative and we need a talented creative professional to match that energy and bring our story to life across all platforms.



About This Role

We are looking for a creative powerhouse who can do it all; design stunning visuals, produce engaging videos, write compelling copy, and manage our social media presence end-to-end. You will be the visual voice of STEAMUP Academy and play a major role in growing our brand awareness, attracting new students, and building an engaged online community.



This role is NOT for someone who only schedules posts. We need someone who can create professional-quality content, think strategically about our brand, and deliver scroll-stopping visuals consistently.



Key Responsibilities

Social Media Management

Manage and grow STEAMUP Academy's presence across Instagram,
, TikTok, YouTube, and LinkedIn

Develop and execute a monthly content calendar with consistent, on-brand posting

Write engaging, audience-appropriate captions with strategic hashtags

Monitor comments, DMs, and community engagement, respond and build relationships

Track performance metrics (reach, engagement, follower growth) and provide monthly reports

Stay current with platform trends, algorithm changes, and viral content formats



Video Production & Editing

- Create professional short-form video content for Reels,
, Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts

- Edit longer-form content such as program highlights, testimonials, and event recaps

- Add captions, graphics, transitions, and music to produce polished, engaging videos

- Create promotional videos and ads for STEAMUP Academy programs and events

- Record and edit basic voiceovers or coordinate with the CEO for video narration



Graphic Design & Visual Content

- Design eye-catching flyers, posters, banners, and promotional materials for programs and events

- Create branded social media graphics, story templates, and carousel posts

- Design
 headers, newsletters, and digital marketing assets

- Produce presentation decks and pitch materials that reflect STEAMUP Academy's brand

- Ensure all visuals maintain consistent branding (colors, fonts, tone)



Marketing & Advertising

- Assist with planning and executing paid ad campaigns 
/Instagram Ads, Google Ads)

- Create content to support student enrollment, event registration, and partnership outreach

- Write basic marketing copy for
s, landing pages, and promotional materials

- Research competitors and emerging trends in STEAM education marketing

- Support community outreach initiatives with digital and print marketing collateral





Required Skills & Experience

Must Have:



- Minimum 2 years of experience in social media management and content creation

- Expert-level proficiency in Canva Pro (portfolio of work required)

- Proficiency in video editing software; CapCut, Adobe Premiere, DaVinci Resolve, or similar

- Strong understanding of Instagram,
, TikTok, and YouTube best practices

- Excellent English writing skills; captions, copy, and communications must be flawless

- An eye for design: you understand composition, color theory, typography, and branding

- Ability to create content that is both visually beautiful AND strategically effective

- Self-motivated, highly organized, and able to hit deadlines without reminders



Preferred / Big Plus:

- Experience with Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro)

- Experience running
/Instagram Ads or Google Ads

- Familiarity with
 marketing platforms (Mailchimp, Flodesk, or similar)

- Experience with scheduling tools (Later, Buffer, Hootsuite, Meta Business Suite)

- Video content creation experience using a smartphone or basic camera setup

- Background or interest in education, youth programs, or nonprofits

- Experience with YouTube SEO and growing a YouTube channel



You Are a Great Fit If You...

- Have a portfolio that makes people stop and say "Wow."

- Understand what makes content go viral and can replicate it with purpose.

- Think like a marketer AND an artist at the same time.

- Are organized enough to execute a content calendar without being chased.

- Take feedback well and iterate quickly.

- Are passionate about education, youth development, or community impact.

- Communicate clearly in writing and always keep the CEO informed on progress.



What We Offer

- Competitive pay: 3.00 – $5.00 USD/hour (based on experience and portfolio quality)

- Full-time, stable, long-term position

- Creative freedom within a supportive and collaborative team

- Opportunity to build an impressive portfolio with a mission-driven brand

- Professional growth in a fast-growing educational organization

- Supportive and inspiring work culture



How to Apply

Your application MUST include ALL of the following or it will not be reviewed:



1. Start your message with the phrase: "I Create for STEAM" (confirms you read the full post)

2. A brief introductory video about yourself and your creative background in your own words)

3. A portfolio link or examples of your work; this is REQUIRED. Include:

- At least 2 social media graphics or flyers you have designed

- At least 1 video you have edited or created

- Any social media accounts you have managed (share the handle)



4. The tools and software you use most (be specific; Canva, CapCut, Adobe, etc.)

5. Your availability, time zone, and internet speed (a fast, stable connection is required)

6. Your resume or CV



Applications without a portfolio will not be considered. We want to see what you can do; show us your best work!

title: Growth / CRO Operator (Ecommerce)
description:
We’re looking for a part-time Growth / CRO Operator to help us improve our ecommerce funnel through structured A/B testing.



We run a fast-growing skincare brand focused on acne and sensitive skin, primarily selling in Europe. This role is about increasing revenue by testing and optimizing key parts of the funnel — not just design, but offers, messaging, and conversion.



You will be responsible for running A/B tests across landing pages, product pages, offers and pricing, as well as
 flows and upsells. Your goal is to identify what drives higher conversion, AOV, and overall revenue per visitor, and iterate quickly based on results.



We’re looking for someone with experience in DTC ecommerce who understands direct response marketing, customer psychology, and how to think beyond surface-level changes. You should be comfortable working with data, but also able to move fast and test ideas without overcomplicating things.



Knowledge of the German language is a very big plus, as our main market is German-speaking and understanding customer language and messaging is important. If you read to this point, include the word “strawberry” in your application to show that you actually read and understand the role.



This is a part-time role (around 10–20 hours per week) with a fixed payment and potential performance-based bonuses. If you’re interested, please share a few examples of your work.

title: Remote Inside Sales Agent (Full-Time, PST Hours)
description:
**Work From Anywhere | Join a Fast-Growing Real Estate Investment Firm**



Are you a persuasive communicator who thrives in inside sales? Do you enjoy qualifying leads, setting high-quality appointments, and driving deals forward through consistent follow-up?



If you’re looking for a long-term remote career with a company that values growth, accountability, and integrity — this opportunity is for you.



About the Role

Position: Inside Sales Agent (ISA)

Location: Anywhere – 100% Remote

Schedule: Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM–5:00 PM Pacific Time

(Occasional weekend work up to 4 hours)

Work Hours: 40 hours/week



As an Inside Sales Agent, you’ll be the frontline sales professional responsible for handling inbound and outbound leads, qualifying motivated sellers, and setting appointments for our acquisitions team. You’ll play a critical role in driving revenue by ensuring only high-quality opportunities move forward.



You’ll work closely with a supportive, performance-driven team that values consistency, ownership, and continuous improvement.



Qualifications & Requirements 

* Minimum 6 months of experience as an Inside Sales Agent (REQUIRED)

* Experience handling high-volume inbound and outbound calls

* Background in real estate, wholesaling, acquisitions, or property sales is a strong plus

* Proven ability to qualify leads, uncover motivation, and handle objections

* Confident, professional, and persuasive phone presence

* Excellent English communication skills (clear, neutral accent preferred)

* Strong follow-up skills and ability to nurture leads over time

* Comfortable working with CRM systems, dialers, and lead management tools

* Highly organized, detail-oriented, and consistent with data entry and notes

* Self-motivated, reliable, and able to hit daily/weekly call and appointment targets

* Team-oriented mindset with a strong sense of accountability

* Positive attitude and genuine interest in building rapport and long-term relationships





Technical Requirements

* Reliable high-speed internet (minimum 50 Mbps) with backup connection

* Reliable laptop/desktop and smartphone

* Quiet, distraction-free workspace suitable for sales calls





About SV Ventures

SV Ventures is a California-based real estate investment firm specializing in residential fix-and-flip projects and new construction throughout the Bay Area. We manage the full lifecycle — acquisitions, design, renovations, and financing — and we’re growing rapidly.



Our culture is built on strong core values:

-Growth Mindset – We learn, adapt, and improve continuously

-Accountability – We take ownership and deliver results

-Relationships – We build trust through every interaction

-Transparency – We value honesty, integrity, and clear communication



If these values resonate with you, you’ll feel right at home here.



Compensation & Benefits

-Hourly Rate: $6–8/hour (based on experience)

-3th Month Pay Bonus (with the opportunity to earn beyond for exceptional performance)

-Property Sale Bonus: $50 per property sold (Average: 12–20 properties per year)

-Bi-weekly Pay

-Long-term, stable remote position with growth opportunities





How to Apply

Please submit the following:



1. Resume (PDF or Word format)

2. Screenshot of Internet Speed Test

3. Computer Specs (make, model, RAM, storage, year of manufacture)

4. 3-minute video (Loom or YouTube only) answering:

      -Tell us about yourself — your experience, education, and why you’re a strong fit for an Inside Sales Agent role

      -What do you believe are the most important qualities of a successful Inside Sales Agent?





Ready to grow with us?

Apply now and become part of a team that values your success as much as its own.



Join SV Ventures — where growth, accountability, and opportunity meet.

title: Case Manager - Paralegal
description:
About Your Startup Operations

Your Startup Operations is a boutique agency providing operational services to small businesses. We work primarily with small business owners in the United States and our vision is to give power to the people who own small businesses by delivering white glove service while maintaining flexibility, care and affordability. 



About the Client

Our client is a California-based employment defense law firm that represents businesses in workplace litigation. This is a lean practice where deadlines are critical, filings are frequent, and the attorney relies heavily on proactive, detail-oriented support. The right candidate will work directly with the lead litigator, gaining hands-on exposure to case management and courtroom preparation, with meaningful opportunities.



Our SCORE Values

Solution-focused: Think critically and propose solutions independently.

Curiosity: Ask questions and actively seek understanding.

Ownership: Take full accountability and responsibility for your role.

Resourcefulness: Use all available resources to solve challenges or answer questions.

Excellence: Deliver high-quality work and exceptional results.



Who You Are

You are highly organized, keeping tasks and deadlines on track.

You thrive in a fast-paced litigation environment and understand the importance of deadlines.

You are proactive- you don’t just wait for instructions, you anticipate what comes next.

You are detail-oriented. Hint: On your first interview, mention which SCORE value you most align with at the end of the interview for huge bonus points.



Role Requirements:

Must have experience working in employment law (ideally in the U.S.)

Must speak and write fluent English

Strong grammar, proofreading, and punctuation skills

Familiarity with CA state court websites, including reservation systems

Knowledge of court and civil-litigation deadlines

Experience in filing pleadings in state courts, including e-filing systems

Experience working in a paperless case/document management system (Clio)

Expert in Microsoft Word, Outlook, and Adobe Pro





Responsibilities:

Create and manage case files in an organized manner within the electronic document management platform (Clio). 

Prepare initial drafts of correspondence, reports, pleadings, motions, and discovery requests/responses. 

Calendar and track litigation deadlines; ensure all tasks are completed in a timely manner. 

Create, file and serve pleadings and discovery, including e-filing and e-service. 

Coordinate and schedule court hearings, depositions, mediations, and other events. 

Review and summarize documents, including discovery responses and deposition transcripts.



Benefits

Full-time, long-term remote position with stable U.S. client work

Paid holidays, sick days and vacation

Ongoing training and process documentation support

Professional development and learning stipends

Workspace improvement stipend

Healthcare reimbursement (after one year of employment)

Performance-based bonuses

Career growth opportunities into senior paralegal or case manager roles

Team-building events and virtual social activities

Supportive, organized, and process-driven work environment



PLEASE APPLY HERE:

title: Short-Form Video Editor & Content Scheduler TikTok
description:
We are a fast-growing dating psychology and coaching brand on YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram. We produce daily short-form content and need a reliable editor who can execute at volume without constant direction.



This is a long-term role for someone who takes pride in their work and wants to grow with a brand that is scaling fast.



What you will do every day:



Cut 20-40 second clips from long-form YouTube videos

Add auto subtitles from tiktok and text overlays from tiktok

Schedule and post 4 videos per day across 2 TikTok accounts (3-hour intervals)

Follow a brand style guide for fonts, colors, and text placement consistently

The dead space will already be cut out of the videos, you just select the pieces of the video to cut out and crop it to the dimensions required



You are the right fit if:



You have edited short-form content specifically for TikTok — not just general video editing

You understand pacing, hook cuts, and retention-based editing

You are detail-oriented and can follow a style guide exactly

You communicate proactively if something is unclear

Your English writing is clear and professional



You are not the right fit if:



You need step-by-step direction on every task

You have only edited long-form or YouTube content

You have missed deadlines in previous roles



Tools we use:



TikTok for editing and scheduling

Google Drive and youtube for file sharing



You can work any hours you want but you must schedule in PST time and be available for discussion once per week as a check in



To be considered you must include:



2–3 examples of TikTok short-form content you have edited

A brief description of your editing process from raw footage to final post

Your availability (hours per day and time zone)



Applications without video examples will not be reviewed.

title: Administrative Virtual Assistant – E-commerce Company (Canada)
description:
About Us: 

We are a growing e-commerce and brand distribution company based in Canada. We are expanding our operations and looking for a reliable and organized Administrative Virtual Assistant to join our small, remote team.



Position Overview:

This is a part-time administrative support role. You will work directly with the business owner to handle a wide variety of administrative, operational, and personal tasks, freeing up the owner to focus on business growth.



Responsibilities:



Bookkeeping and Invoices:

Assist with invoice tracking, basic bookkeeping tasks, and expense reconciliation.

Maintain accurate financial records and flag any discrepancies.



 and Communications:

Manage and triage business
 inboxes, flagging urgent items and drafting routine responses.

Communicate with suppliers and brand contacts on behalf of the business owner.

Maintain records of communications and follow up on outstanding items.



What is one thing you enjoy doing outside of work?

Reporting and Data Entry:

Maintain weekly and monthly reports on business operations.

Update spreadsheets and internal trackers with accurate data.

Upload documents to cloud storage as needed.



Back-End Operations Support:

Manage open cases and tickets with online marketplaces, follow up and escalate when needed.

Monitor inventory health and coordinate inbound shipments.

Track and action shipment discrepancies and missing or damaged items. When submitting your application, please use the following subject line: VA Application - [Your Name] - [Your Favourite Food]. Applications that do not follow this format will not be considered.



Marketplace Listings:

title: Outreach
description:
I need help with guerrilla marketing and soft outreach.



What I mean by guerrilla marketing:

I do not mean paid ads.

I mean low-cost, smart actions that build familiarity and attention online in a natural way.



For us, that means:



* finding good-fit people

* watching how they present themselves

* liking and understanding their content first

* leaving thoughtful comments when appropriate

* replying to stories in a natural way

* sending soft messages that do not feel like a pitch

* following up without pressure

* creating familiarity before trying to talk business



The goal is not to spam people.

The goal is not to sell in the first message.

The goal is to lower resistance, feel human, and start real conversations.



People are guarded with cold outreach, so we do not want messages that feel obvious, needy, or salesy.

We want messages that feel personal, calm, relevant, and easy to reply to.



Who we are trying to reach:



* coaches

* consultants

* service providers



What we help with:

We build client management systems like websites, booking calendars, payment systems, CRM, and automations so their business can run more smoothly and not depend on them doing everything manually.



How to do the assignment:



1. Find people who look like a good fit

2. Study their page for a minute before messaging

3. Look for something real to mention

4. Start with familiarity, not a pitch

5. Keep the first message short

6. Make the message about them, not us

7. Only move deeper if they respond

8. Track who you contacted, who replied, and who is interested



Examples of guerrilla marketing actions:



* reply to a story with a short real reaction

* comment on a post with something thoughtful, not generic

* engage with 1 to 3 pieces of content before messaging

* send a message based on something specific from their page

* follow up later in a calm way if they do not reply



Do NOT send messages like:



* “Do you need more leads?”

* “I can help grow your business”

* “Are you struggling with…”

* anything that sounds copy-paste or like a hard pitch



Use softer message styles like these:



Story reply examples:



* “That was a solid point.”

* “You explained that really well.”

* “I like how clear your message is.”

* “That’s a strong way to put it.”



DM examples:



* “Hey [Name], came across your page and you’ve built a strong presence.”

* “Hey [Name], I checked out your content. The way you explain what you do is clear.”

* “Hey [Name], your page feels sharp and intentional. That stood out.”

* “Hey [Name], I was looking through your page and you’ve clearly put real work into your brand.”



If they reply, then move naturally into curiosity:



* “I was actually curious about something — do you still handle a lot of your backend manually?”

* “You look well positioned on the front end. I was curious whether your systems behind the scenes are automated yet or still pretty hands-on.”

* “A lot of people with strong brands still run booking, follow-up, and payments very manually. I was not sure if that was true for you too.”

* “You seem to have the front side dialed in. Is the backend of the business smooth too, or is a lot of it still manual?”



If the conversation opens up, then you can say:



* “That’s actually what we help with. We build client management systems that handle things like website, booking, payments, CRM, and automations so the business runs smoother.”

* “We help set up the backend side so business owners are not stuck doing everything themselves.”



Important:

The emotion we want to tap into is not pressure.

It is recognition.

People want to feel seen, understood, respected, and not sold.

So the message should make them feel:



* noticed

* understood

* safe to reply

* not pressured



That is the assignment:

Use smart, soft outreach and guerrilla marketing to create familiarity, start conversations, and find interested people without sounding like cold outreach.

title: Full Time Remote Paralegal and Client Support for Social Security Disability Law Firm
description:
We are a U.S.-based law firm focused exclusively on Social Security Disability cases at both the agency level and in Federal Court (U.S. District Court).



We are hiring a full-time remote paralegal based in the Philippines who can handle both case work and client communication, including outbound calls.

This is a long-term role for someone who is highly organized, detail-oriented, and has impeccable English communication skills.



What You’ll Be Doing

This role combines legal support, operations, and client communication.

Social Security Disability (SSD) Case Support

Review medical records and identify key evidence

Prepare medical summaries and timelines

Organize large administrative records (often 1,000+ pages)

Track deadlines for Appeals Council and Federal Court cases

Assist with document preparation and filings

Operations & Case Management

Maintain detailed case tracking systems (Excel / Google Sheets)

Monitor case statuses and ensure deadlines are met

Keep internal systems accurate and up to date

Help improve workflows and processes

Client Communication (Outbound Calls Required)

Make outbound calls to clients to follow up on cases, documents, and next steps

Communicate clearly, professionally, and confidently with U.S.-based clients

Explain basic case status updates (training provided)

Document all communications accurately



This Job Is For You if:

You have near-perfect English (written and spoken)

Sound clear, professional, and confident on phone calls

Are extremely detail-oriented and organized

Can switch between deep focus work and client interaction

Take ownership of your work and follow through

Are comfortable handling sensitive client situations with professionalism



Required Skills

Excellent spoken English (neutral accent, easy to understand)

Strong written English (grammar and clarity matter)

Experience with outbound calls or client communication

Strong Excel, Word, and Office 365 skills

Ability to manage multiple deadlines and priorities

Reliable internet and ability to work U.S. business hours (8:30 am to 5:30 pm Eastern Time)



Bonus Skills (Preferred but Not Required)

Experience working with U.S. law firms

Social Security Disability experience

Experience summarizing medical records

Familiarity with CRMs or case management systems (Clio Manage, Clio Draft, Lawmatics, Zoom, Teams, etc.)



Why This Role Is Different

You will be involved in both legal work and client interaction

Your work directly impacts real people going through difficult situations

We value precision, organization, and clear communication

Opportunity for long-term growth within the firm



Compensation

Competitive monthly salary (based on experience)

Performance-based increases

Stable, long-term position

American Holidays off

10 Vacation days a year

5 days of sick leave a year



What we will need from you to apply:



A short introduction

A description of your experience with outbound calls

Your experience with Excel or tracking systems

Any legal or medical-related experience

A video recording (3 minutes) introducing yourself and explaining your experience and why you are the best fit for this role

A writing sample. (Do not use AI tools for this response. We are evaluating your natural writing ability).



Prompt 1 for writing Sample:

You are given 1,500 pages of medical records for a Social Security disability case.

Explain how you would review, organize, and summarize the records so that an attorney can quickly understand the key issues.

 

Prompt 2 for writing Sample:

A client has not returned your calls for two weeks and has not submitted the required documents.

Write a message 
 or call script) explaining why the documents are important and encouraging the client to respond.

title: Registered Nurse Generalist – Adult Day Health
description:
Position Title: RN Generalist  

Reports To: Program Manager

Location: Remote



Position Summary

The RN Generalist supports participants enrolled in Adult Day Health by assessing needs, developing individualized service plans, coordinating interdisciplinary care, and ensuring high quality, person centered support. This role maintains continuity of care through effective communication, timely documentation, and active collaboration with caregivers, clinicians, and internal tea
mbers.

 

Essential Functions

Client Assessment & Care Planning

• Determines clients’ needs by establishing rapport with participants and caregivers, identifying goals, and coordinating interdisciplinary assessments.

• Develops and implements individualized care plans for clients with complex needs and chronic conditions. Includes both initial care plans and ongoing care plans quarterly. 

• Adjusts services and care plans based on ongoing client evaluation and progress toward goals.

Care Coordination & Communication

• Maintains continuity of care by documenting and communicating actions and updates in accordance with WAC requirements and Full Life Care policies.

• Communicates changes in client condition to internal tea
mbers, caregivers, and physicians in a timely manner.

• Participates in telehealth services, including virtual visits and phone communication with clients and caregivers.

• Requests medical reports and orders for new enrollees and annually thereafter.

• Requests annual care plan signature from client/guardian.

• Sends updated care plans to client’s DSHS or VA case manager. 

• Follows up with new enrollees to plan start of care at Adult Day Health. 





Team Collaboration

• Works closely with the nurses, social worker, intake specialist, program manager and director to coordinate client services.

• Participates in interdisciplinary meetings and contributes to care planning, problem solving, and client advocacy.

Documentation & Compliance

• Maintains accurate, complete, and timely case management records in compliance with agency policies and Washington Administrative Code (WAC) requirements.

• Completes required attendance documentation and inputs monthly data into Cadcare.

• Reconciles client’s medication in Cadcare. 

Administrative Support

• Answers incoming calls, provides information to caregivers and providers, and schedules site visits.

• Manages required documentation and updates to ensure regulatory compliance and operational efficiency.

• Assists with submitting Access transportation applications and follow through 

• Complete projects and tasks assigned by supervisor;

 

Qualifications

• RN licensure

• Experience working with older adults or individuals with disabilities strongly preferred.

• Strong communication, organization, and documentation skills.

• Ability to work effectively within a multidisciplinary team.

• Proficiency in electronic record systems; Cadcare experience a plus.

title: YouTube Video Editor & Thumbnail Designer Needed (Part-Time / On-Call / Long-Term)
description:
I am currently looking for a reliable and talented YouTube video editor to help with my content on an on-call / as-needed basis.



This is a long-term position, but it is not full-time and hours are not guaranteed every week. Work will be assigned only when new videos are ready. This is a great fit for someone who already has ongoing clients and is looking to add another steady client with clear expectations and long-term potential.



I create educational and informational YouTube content, and I need an editor who understands how to make videos more engaging, more polished, and easier to watch without making them feel over-edited or distracting.



Type of Content

You will mainly be editing:



Talking-head YouTube videos

Educational, business, and informational content

Videos that need strong pacing, clarity, and professional presentation



The editing style should feel clean, modern, and engaging. I am not looking for excessive effects or overly flashy edits. The goal is to improve viewer experience and retention while keeping the content professional.



Your Responsibilities



Video Editing



Remove mistakes, pauses, filler words, and unnecessary sections

Tighten pacing to improve watch time and retention

Add clean cuts, zooms, light transitions, and simple motion effects

Add background music when appropriate

Add text, visual callouts, or simple graphics when needed

Follow editing instructions and keep a consistent style

Complete assigned projects on time



Thumbnail Design



Create professional and attention-grabbing YouTube thumbnails

Use strong contrast, bold layouts, and readable text

Make thumbnails optimized for both desktop and mobile

Keep designs clean and in line with the brand style



Ideal Applicant



Minimum 2 years of YouTube editing experience

Understands YouTube retention and pacing

Has edited talking-head or educational videos before

Can create strong thumbnails that improve click-through rate

Good English comprehension

Communicates well and responds in a timely manner

Detail-oriented and able to follow directions

Open to feedback and willing to make improvements

Can work independently and stay organized



Technical Requirements



Reliable internet connection

Quiet home-based workspace

Computer or laptop capable of handling video editing smoothly

Willing to use TimeProof for tracking time worked



Position Details



Pay: $3 USD per hour

Schedule: On-call / as needed

Hours: Vary depending on how many videos are being produced

Location: Remote

Payment: PayPal



How to Apply

Please send:



Links to videos you have personally edited

A short summary of your experience

The editing software you use

Thumbnail samples if available



To show that you read this job post carefully, begin your application with the word: RETENTION



This position is for someone who wants a long-term client relationship, communicates clearly, and takes pride in delivering quality work consistently.



I’m looking for someone dependable, easy to work with, and committed to doing great work over the long run.

 capture, upsells, bundles, or other conversion tools

Requirements:

Experience with e-commerce conversion best practices

Experience organizing products and collections properly

Strong attention to detail

title: Operations Associate (Remote)
description:
The Operations Associate will be a core structure of our Operations Team. The role requires strong organizational skills, creative problem solving, and extraordinary time management skills. Luminary Travel is a fast paced environment with lots of daily changes that require adaptability. It is very important to have the ability to manage stressful situations and execute detailed tasks in a calm and controlled manner. The Operations Associate is the key to making sure that our trips are planned/executed meticulously and go off without a hitch. We are looking for someone who enjoys travel planning and is able to view trips from both the Clients eyes and the Travel Planners point of view to make decisions that are always best for our clients.

Minimum of two (2) years of work experience

Exceptional organizational skills with the ability to multitask

Exceptional listening skills with the ability to quickly identify issues and proactively solve problems

Tech-savvy and comfortable with navigating web-based reservation programs, helpdesk software, internet searches, airline websites/apps, SMS messaging, and smartphone apps

Dependable and flexible with work schedule

Proven ability to work independently with minimal supervision in a fast-paced environment

A passionate approach to customer service, positive attitude, and strong work ethic

Professional verbal, and written communication skills using appropriate grammar, terminology, tone, cadence, and pronunciation

Must be able to work various shifts including irregular shifts, holidays, and weekends

Knowledge of Salesforce and Axus softwares

s, and text messages in a professional and timely manner

Manage and prioritize a lengthy queue of daily/weekly tasks within Salesforce’s task list

Send daily reminders of upcoming services via
 and text to clients and concierge

Exhibit utmost confidentiality with clients and client information

Accept and accurately process credit card payments and invoices for services rendered

Provide excellent customer service to clients

Other adhoc travel planning projects



Compensation & Perks:



Competitive Salary

Generous Healthcare Benefits for US-based employees 

401K Offering for US-based employees

Annual Team Retreat

Ongoing opportunities for professional development

Discounted Hotel Rates and Complimentary nights when available on World Travel

title: Service Admin Coordinator
description:

About Conserva:

Conserva is a forward-thinking company dedicated to administering solar system warranties and services. We pride ourselves on delivering exceptional customer support and high-quality service to our clients, all while fostering an inclusive, remote-first work culture. We are excited to welcome talented professionals to join our dynamic team.



What You'll Do:

Serve as the liaison between our Operations and Service teams — ensuring every ticket is complete, accurate, and routed to the right place before it ever reaches the Service team.

1. Review and process non-revenue-generating tickets and calls, managing them from intake to resolution.

2. Handle customer-facing tickets and calls involving non-revenue discussions, ensuring customers receive timely and accurate support.

3. Validate that all required information from Operations is present and complete before dispatching any ticket to the Service team — incomplete submissions go back, not forward.

4. Maintain accurate, up-to-date records and documentation across our systems, including ClickUp.

5. Coordinate closely with Operations and Service to ensure process compliance and smooth handoffs.

6. Perform quality assurance reviews on completed tickets to catch errors before they cause downstream delays.

7. Support daily administrative workflows and assist with internal tasks as needed.



What We're Looking For:

1. Strong organizational skills and exceptional attention to detail — you'll be the last line of defense before a ticket moves forward.

2. Comfortable working independently in a process-driven, behind-the-scenes coordination role.

3. Excellent written English and the ability to follow and enforce structured workflows.

4. Experience with project management or CRM tools (ClickUp experience is a plus).

5. A reliable internet connection, quiet workspace, and the self-discipline to thrive in a remote environment.

title: Operations Coordinator/ Business Developer
description:
Operations Coordinator & Business Development — US Painting & Drywall Contractor (Remote, Full-Time)



Company: Waterside Painters LLC — Tampa, FL based painting and drywall subcontractor serving general contractors, builders, and restoration companies across Florida.



Pay: $800-1,000/month depending on experience. Performance review and raise at 90 days.



Hours: Monday-Friday, 9am-5pm US Eastern Time. Must be available during these hours for live phone calls with US-based clients.



About the role:



You will be the operational backbone of a growing contracting business. This is not a basic VA role — you will manage the full lifecycle of projects from first contact with a potential client through job completion and payment. You are replacing a previous tea
mber and inheriting an active pipeline of companies in various stages of engagement. This role has room to grow into a full operations manager position as the business scales into new markets.

What you'll do every day:



* Follow up with warm leads via phone and
 to push them toward giving us work

* Add 20 new companies per week to our CRM pipeline through Google Maps research

* Send bid packets (insurance certificates, company info, W-9) to new clients

* Receive blueprints and project plans from clients requesting quotes

* Use our internal estimating tools to generate scopes of work and pricing documents

* Send quotes to clients and follow up on their response

* Coordinate subcontractors — scheduling them for job site visits, confirming start dates, communicating scope details

* Track active jobs — what phase they're in, any issues, what's next

* Generate and send invoices when work is completed

* Follow up on unpaid invoices

* Track subcontractor payments and flag when subs need to be paid

* Maintain and update HubSpot CRM daily

* Maintain a master tracking spreadsheet with all jobs, margins, payment status

* Manage
 inbox — respond to routine items, flag urgent items for owner review

* Handle document filing and organization in Google Drive



Requirements:

* Fluent English — you will be making phone calls to American business owners. Accent must be neutral enough to sound natural on the phone. This is non-negotiable.

* Experience with CRM software (HubSpot preferred but not required)

* Comfortable with Google Workspace and Microsoft Excel

* Reliable internet and quiet environment for phone calls

* Self-starter who doesn't need to be told what to do next — you see what needs to happen and you do it

* Available to start within 1 week



Strong plus:

* Spanish fluency (many of our subcontractors are Spanish-speaking)

* Experience in construction, property management, or real estate industry

* Experience with cold calling or business development



Do not apply if you need to be told what to do at every step. This role requires someone who can own a process end to end with minimal supervision.



Trial period: 2 weeks paid trial before full commitment.



To apply: Send a 60-second voice recording introducing yourself in English. Tell me about your current or most recent role and what you're looking for in your next position. If you have Spanish fluency, include 30 seconds in Spanish. Applications without a voice recording will not be reviewed.

title: Paralegal Assistant
description:
We are a growing law firm based in Cleveland, Ohio, USA, focused on representing families in nursing home abuse and neglect lawsuits. We are hiring a full-time Paralegal Assistant to support our legal team in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.



This is a great role for someone who is highly detail-oriented, organized, responsive, and able to thrive under a heavy workload. The right person for this position enjoys research, keeps track of moving parts, communicates well with clients and professionals, and takes pride in making sure important tasks and deadlines do not fall through the cracks.



You do not need to have prior law firm experience to succeed here. We will train the right person. What matters most is that you are smart, coachable, dependable, and able to manage a high volume of work with accuracy and professionalism.



If you are the kind of person who likes structure, learns quickly, communicates clearly, and enjoys keeping complex matters organized, this may be a strong fit.



What You’ll Be Responsible For

- Supporting attorneys, paralegals, and tea
mbers with case-related tasks and follow-up

- Managing and tracking multiple deadlines, priorities, and moving pieces across active cases

- Conducting research and gathering information to support case development

- Assisting with client onboarding and helping new clients through the intake process

- Communicating with clients clearly, professionally, and compassionately

- Keeping clients informed proactively and responding to case status questions

- Saving, organizing, and filing digital documents according to firm systems and file naming conventions

- Managing case tasks and helping ensure deadlines are met

- Scheduling events involving lawyers, witnesses, clients, experts, court reporters, and other parties

- Managing lawyers’ calendars for case-specific deadlines and events

- Reviewing, organizing, and auditing medical records and other case documents

- Assisting with drafting and responding to discovery

- Communicating with courts, clerks’ offices, opposing counsel, clients, and related parties

- Handling other case-management and administrative tasks as needed



This Role Is a Good Fit If You Are

- highly detail-oriented and naturally organized

- able to handle a heavy workload without losing track of details

- strong in written and spoken English

- professional, clear, and confident in client communication

- comfortable managing multiple deadlines and tasks at once

- dependable and responsive

- willing to follow systems carefully while staying flexible as processes improve

- coachable and open to regular feedback

- growth-oriented and serious about personal and professional development

- proactive and willing to solve problems, not just report them

- comfortable learning new software and working in a fully digital environment



Required Qualifications

- 2+ years of experience in roles involving data management, client communication, and administrative support

- Strong attention to detail and organizational ability

- Experience managing multiple tasks, deadlines, and follow-up items

- Strong written and verbal English communication skills

- Reliable high-speed internet

- Quiet, professional remote work environment



Preferred Qualifications

- Experience in a law firm, medical office, insurance setting, or other detail-heavy environment

- Experience with research, records organization, or deadline-driven case or project support

- Experience working remotely with U.S.-based teams



Software / Technical Skills

- You should be comfortable using and learning cloud-based systems and digital tools, including:

- Google Workspace and/or Microsoft Office

-
 and calendar systems

- Zoom

- Cloud-based file storage and document management platforms

- Case management systems

- Databases and spreadsheets

- Other paperless, cloud-based tools used in a remote law firm environment



Compensation and Growth

- Base pay starts at $6.00 per hour

- Discretionary performance bonuses based on both firm performance and personal performance measures

Advancement opportunities with increased pay and responsibilities are available to top performers after 12 months



Schedule

- Full-time, 6 AM to 3 PM EST 

- Expected average of 40 hours per week



Location

- Remote / Virtual

- We are a remote, cloud-based firm

Our main office is located in Cleveland, Ohio, United States



About Our Work Environment

We are a positive, growth-oriented firm that values accountability, work ethic, problem-solving, and continuous improvement. We provide systems for success, encourage learning, and reward strong performance. People who thrive here are energized by responsibility, willing to learn, and committed to doing excellent work as part of a team.



Training and Experience

You do not need to know how to do everything already. We will train you on our systems and processes. Prior legal experience is not required. We are more interested in finding someone who is adaptable, capable, and willing to learn than someone who is set in doing things their own way.



Important Application Instructions

Please note that applications for this position will only be accepted through Onlinejobs.ph. We do not consider or review applications submitted through other job platforms, including follow-ups from external sites, such as 
 or
. Applications submitted outside of our designated platform will not be considered.



We require all candidates to complete a brief IQ and English proficiency test. These tests are a formal requirement and must be taken through OnlineJobsPH. Completing these assessments is essential for us to ensure that all applicants have the necessary skills for success in the role. Please provide a brief video introduction explaining why you want to work with our firm.  



Please make sure to complete these tests before moving forward in the application process. You can access the tests using the following link: 
/testcontent

title: EXPERIENCED PARALEGAL
description:
Join Our Compassionate & Collaborative Team: Full-Time Virtual ParalegalThe Paraclete Law Center, LLC.



Life is too short to start your day with dread. At The Paraclete Law Center, we understand that legal issues can be overwhelming and often create significant stress for our clients. Our firm is committed to easing our client’s  burdens: we thoroughly analyze the complexities of their matters and develop sound, comprehensive strategies to pursue the favorable outcome they deserve so they can move forward with confidence. 



 We guide clients through a transitional time of remarkable growth and transformation, supporting them in a manner consistent with their values using non-adversarial techniques when possible. We are committed to a firm culture where every tea
mber is valued and invested in, working collaboratively, strategically, and creatively to bring cases toward resolution.



We are seeking a highly motivated, dedicated, and experienced Virtual Paralegal to join our growing legal team. This is a full-time, 40-hour per week remote position where you will manage a caseload from inception to closure. This role requires a self-starter with exceptional organizational and communication skills, ready to take initiative with minimal hand-holding. Preference will be given to candidates with experience in Family Law and Probate Litigation.-----Key Responsibilities

Legal Document Preparation: Draft, proofread, and meticulously format a wide range of legal documents and correspondence, including pleadings, motions, discovery requests, affidavits, and agreements, ensuring grammatical accuracy and legal precision.

Case Management & Organization: Manage case files, ensuring all documents are properly filed, and all deadlines are met with 100% accuracy. Maintain and update the firm’s case management system.

Client & Stakeholder Communication: Serve as a primary point of contact for clients, providing regular case updates and ensuring they feel valued and supported. Coordinate with opposing counsel and court personnel as needed.

Research & Case Development: Conduct basic legal research using various databases to compile relevant information. Initiate and follow up on requests for medical/employment records and perform thorough background checks.

Time Management & Billing: LOG ALL TIME CONTEMPORANEOUSLY, providing a clear and precise record of hours spent on each task and case. Log a minimum of 25 hours per week dedicated to paralegal duties.

Administrative Support: Assist with filing, scanning, and organizing legal documents electronically and handle virtual mail and courier deliveries.

Compensation & Benefits





We offer a highly competitive compensation package commensurate with experience, as well as opportunities for ongoing professional development and training. Our tea
mbers also enjoy generous paid time off to ensure a healthy work-life balance.

Qualifications

Experience: Prior experience as a paralegal is required, preferably in family law and probate litigation.

Communication: Fluent in English, with excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Skills: Strong organizational skills, impeccable attention to detail, and the ability to multitask and manage time effectively in a fast-paced, remote environment.

Mindset: A positive attitude, commitment to exceptional customer service, and the proven ability to work independently and take initiative.

Technical Proficiency: Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite and comfortable learning new case management software.

Virtual Setup: Reliable internet connection and a quiet workspace.

How to Apply



We are excited to learn more about your qualifications and experience. To help us better understand your suitability for this role, we kindly request that you provide the following two items as part of your application:

Writing Sample: Please submit a recent writing sample (memo, brief, or other document) that showcases your legal research, drafting, and communication skills.

Video Introduction (2-3 minutes): Create a brief video introducing yourself and explaining why you believe you would be an excellent fit for our firm. We are particularly interested in hearing about your:

Motivation: Why you are interested in this position and what attracts you to working in family law and probate litigation.

Relevant Experience: Examples of your organizational skills and how you have managed tasks and deadlines.

Skills & Strengths: Focus on your customer service abilities, time management, and self-motivation.

Problem-Solving: Share an example of a challenging situation you faced professionally and how you resolved it.

Professionalism: Explain how you maintain professionalism and ensure high-quality work in a virtual setting.

We look forward to welcoming a new member to our team who shares our passion for helping families and providing outstanding legal services.

title: Medical Virtual Assistant (Philippine RN Preferred)
description:

This role is best suited for a Philippine-licensed Registered Nurse (RN) or someone with a strong healthcare administrative background who is comfortable working in a fast-paced medical support environment.



This is a non-clinical administrative support role. The ideal candidate should be professional, dependable, and able to handle healthcare-related workflows with accuracy and efficiency.



Responsibilities



Responsibilities may include:



Uploading and organizing patient documents into the medical record

Assisting with provider dictations and transcription support

Monitoring and clearing fax inboxes

Reviewing incoming documents for proper routing

Managing
 correspondence and administrative communication

Answering inbound phone calls professionally

Taking accurate messages and escalating urgent matters appropriately

Supporting provider and office workflows as assigned

Assisting with general healthcare administrative support tasks

Preferred Qualifications

Active or previous Registered Nurse (RN) licensure in the Philippines strongly preferred

Ideal Candidate

mbers

title: Bookkeeping (Xero, Excel) + Guest Communications STR (Airbnb)
description:
ARE YOU GREAT WITH NUMBERS AND PEOPLE?



We’re offering a long-term VA role that combines bookkeeping + admin (Xero & Excel) with guest/cleaner communication for an Airbnb property business in New Zealand.

 • Paid fortnightly in USD ($297.00)

 • Morning PH hours (no overnights!)

 • Mix of admin + on-call hours (you’re not desk-bound during the on-call hours)

 • 13th-month pay provided



EXPERIENCED VIRTUAL ASSISTANT (BOOKKEEPING + GUEST COMMUNICATIONS)



MAC Property Ltd is a boutique short-term rental management company (Airbnb) based in New Zealand. We manage multiple properties and pride ourselves on delivering seamless guest experiences and efficient operations.



This role is a mix of set administration hours and on-call message hours: during admin hours you’ll handle a full range of bookkeeping and admin tasks (plus guest/cleaner communications as they come in), while during on-call hours you’ll be available within 10 minutes to handle guest or cleaner messages and emergencies as they arise.



THE ROLE



 • Administration hours: General admin (extremely varied and constantly changing, ranging from recruitment to supply orders) and bookkeeping tasks, while also juggling guest and cleaner communications as they come in. You’ll need to enjoy switching gears -  picking up messages as they arrive without minding the interruptions -  then smoothly jumping back into your tasks. Paid at USD $5.50.00 per hour.

 • On-call message hours: Respond within 10 minutes. Focused purely on guest and cleaner communication, including handling emergencies as they arise (e.g., a missed clean or a faulty dishwasher). These hours are paid at 60% of the full hourly rate (USD $5.50), which equals USD $3.30 per hour. On-call hours can be done while you’re out and about — you’re not tied to a desk - but you’ll need to be comfortable staying alert and switching quickly to deal with messages as they come in. Sometimes there may be no messages for several hours, other times you might need to spend an hour resolving an unexpected issue.



WEEKLY SCHEDULE (NZ TIME)



The schedule is shown in New Zealand time. Applicants must be comfortable converting this to their local time, including during daylight saving changes.



• Monday: 10:00am – 3:00pm (Admin) + 3:00pm – 10:30pm (Message Monitoring)

• Tuesday: Off

• Wednesday: 10:00am – 1:30pm (Admin)

• Thursday: 10:00am – 1:30pm (Admin)

• Friday: 10:00am – 1:30pm (Admin)

• Saturday: Off

• Sunday: 10:00am – 10:30pm (Message Monitoring)



Important: Dec 15 to Jan 15 is our peak season, so no additional time off is permitted outside your normal days off. One 1–2 day break may be arranged with prior approval.



WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR



 • Strong bookkeeping experience 

 • Very strong Excel skills (reporting, reconciling, managing data)

 • Confident with Google Drive (Docs, Sheets, file organisation, sharing links)

 • Friendly, confident, professional communicator

 • Able to be assertive and persuasive (nicely) with cleaners or guests

 • Excellent administration and organisational skills

 • Happy to pick up the phone via WhatsApp if needed

 • Must supply your own MacBook (Windows will not work with our systems) and phone with strong data coverage



WHY JOIN US?



 • Supportive and professional team

 • A role that’s varied, interesting, and people-focused

 • Long-term, stable role with guaranteed fortnightly USD pay + 13th month pay



APPLICATIONS



We want to get to know YOU.



Please send us a message via this platform and include two Google Drive links (ensure sharing is set to “anyone with the link”):

1. A personalised cover letter (PDF format, addressed to MAC Property) including:

        - Why this role appealed to you

        - Details of your specific experience that match our requirements

2. Your CV (PDF format)



We look forward to hearing from you — many thanks.

title: Senior AWS DevOps Engineer – AWS Cost Optimization & Kubernetes
description:
Job Description:

About the Role

We're a blockchain/DeFi company running production infrastructure that's bleeding money unnecessarily. Our AWS bill is sitting at $4,500/month – and we know it shouldn't be anywhere near that. We need someone who can come in, audit the whole setup, and actually fix it. Not just give us a report that sits in a folder.



We've already been through a few candidates who talked a big game but couldn't execute. We're done with that. If you're the type who needs handholding or can't work independently, this isn't for you.





What You'll Actually Do

- Audit our entire AWS infrastructure and identify exactly where we're wasting money (we expect significant savings)

- Optimize our Kubernetes clusters – right-size nodes, fix resource requests/limits, implement autoscaling that actually works

- Implement cost controls: Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, spot instances where appropriate, and cleanup of orphaned resources

- Set up monitoring and alerts so costs don't creep back up without us knowing

- Document everything you do so the team can maintain it





Hard Requirements (Non-Negotiable)

- 3+ years hands-on Kubernetes experience in production environments – EKS specifically preferred

- Deep AWS knowledge: EC2, EKS, RDS, S3, CloudWatch, Cost Explorer, Trusted Advisor

- Proven track record of actually reducing AWS costs (be ready to share numbers/percentages)

- Strong scripting skills (Python, Bash) for automation

- Infrastructure as Code: Terraform or CloudFormation (Terraform preferred)

- Self-starter who can take ownership and execute without constant oversight





!!!!CRITICAL: This Role Requires Surgical Precision

This is production infrastructure running live systems. Think of this like surgery – one wrong move can take everything down.

- NO cowboy moves. You don't delete, modify, or remove anything without a clear plan first.

- Every change must be planned out, documented, and confirmed with the CEO before execution.

- Extreme attention to detail. You double-check and triple-check before touching anything.

- If you're not 100% sure what something does, you ask first. You don't assume.

- Methodical execution only. We value careful and correct over fast and reckless.





Strong Plus

- Experience with blockchain/Web3 infrastructure

- Karpenter, Cluster Autoscaler, or KEDA experience

-  AWS certifications (Solutions Architect, DevOps Engineer)

- Experience with FinOps tools (Kubecost, CloudHealth, 
)





What We Need In Your Application

Don't send a generic application. We will ignore it. Include:

- Specific examples of AWS cost optimization you've done – what was the setup, what did you change, what % did you save?

- Your Kubernetes experience – cluster sizes you've managed, scaling strategies you've implemented

- Your availability and timezone (we need someone who can overlap with US business hours for some calls)





Compensation & Structure

- $20-25/hour based on experience and proven results

- 20 hours/week to start – potential for more hours for the right person

- Fully remote

- Initial project: Comprehensive audit + implementation plan with clear cost reduction targets





If you're someone who can actually deliver results and not just talk about them – we want to hear from you. Show us what you've done.
