title: Social Media Expert (Instagram & YouTube)
description:
Social Media Expert (Instagram & YouTube)

Part-Time Remote Role | Philippines-Based | TrainerHQ



We're hiring a sharp, proactive Social Media Expert to lead and manage our social media presence across Instagram and YouTube.



This is not a basic admin role. We are looking for someone with genuine expertise in social media strategy, content direction, video editing, posting, performance analysis and growth across both platforms.



Trainer HQ is a business coaching company. We help personal trainers, online coaches, gym owners and studio owners build and grow better businesses. Our content is about business strategy, growth, marketing, sales, pricing and operations, created specifically for fitness professionals. We are not a fitness brand. We are an expert-led education and coaching brand that serves the fitness industry.



Right now, most of our social media content relies on the founders to come up with ideas, organise content and get posts live. As the business grows, that is no longer the best use of founder time.



We need someone who can take ownership of the social media process across both Instagram and YouTube, guide content direction, tell us what content is needed each week, edit and prepare content, and manage the planning, posting and optimisation so our brand shows up consistently and strategically on both channels.



The person we hire needs to be skilled at making business education and thought leadership content compelling, engaging and shareable. Our founders have the knowledge and the expertise. Your job is to help them package and present it in a way that stops the scroll, builds authority and grows our audience of fitness business owners.



What you'll be responsible for



1. Instagram



- Lead the weekly social media content plan for Instagram

- Develop content ideas aligned with our brand, audience and content pillars

- Create strong hooks, captions, post copy and calls to action designed to drive engagement and build authority

- Recommend the right mix of Reels, Stories, feed posts and other content formats

- Tell the founders exactly what content, footage, talking points or insights are needed each week

- Edit video content using raw founder footage, including B-roll, pacing, structure and overall presentation

- Prepare content so it is ready to publish in line with our brand and messaging

- Schedule and publish content consistently so execution does not rely on the founders

- Review analytics and performance data to identify what is working, what is not, and what needs to improve

- Make clear recommendations on content direction, testing and improvements each week



YouTube



- Lead the weekly and monthly content planning for YouTube alongside Instagram

- Develop video concepts and formats aligned with our brand and audience including long-form educational content and YouTube Shorts

- Advise the founders on video topics, talking points, structure and what content to record each week

- Edit long-form YouTube videos using raw founder footage including pacing, structure, B-roll, graphics, chapters and overall presentation

- Repurpose YouTube content for Instagram and repurpose Instagram content into YouTube Shorts where applicable

- Write strong YouTube titles, descriptions, tags and chapter markers optimised for search and discoverability (YouTube SEO)

- Create or direct the creation of thumbnails that are visually compelling and drive click-through

- Manage channel settings including playlists, end screens, cards and community posts

- Review YouTube analytics including watch time, click-through rate, audience retention and subscriber trends and use this data to improve content

- Make clear recommendations on what is working, what to test and what to change



What success looks like in this role



- Our Instagram and YouTube content is planned and executed consistently each week

- The founders are no longer carrying the load of organising, editing and posting content on either platform

- Content is more strategic, more engaging and better positioned to reach fitness business owners

- Business insights, frameworks and founder expertise are packaged in a way that builds credibility and authority

- Video content is edited well and presented in a way that keeps attention on both short-form and long-form

- Hooks, captions, titles and thumbnails improve over time and drive better results

- Content is intelligently repurposed across platforms without duplicating effort

- We see stronger engagement, better reach, better watch time and better audience response across both channels

- Reporting is clear, useful and tied to actual content decisions

- You take initiative and keep things moving without needing to be chased



We're looking for someone who



- Has proven experience managing and growing both Instagram and YouTube accounts strategically

- Understands how to make expert-led, education-based content engaging and compelling on social media

- Can develop content ideas that match a clear target market and brand position

- Can write strong hooks, captions, YouTube titles and post copy in clear, natural English

- Has strong video editing skills and can turn raw founder footage into strong content for both short-form and long-form

- Understands pacing, structure, B-roll, attention retention and what makes short-form and long-form content perform

- Understands YouTube SEO and how to optimise for discoverability and watch time

- Knows how to create or direct thumbnails that drive click-through

- Knows how to interpret metrics across both platforms and use analytics to guide content decisions

- Is organised, proactive and confident in driving a weekly content workflow across two channels

- Can work well with founders and extract content from busy people

- Understands brand voice and can stay consistent with it across platforms

- Has strong attention to detail and follows through



Bonus if you have



- Experience working with coaching, consulting or education brands

- Experience working with founder-led or expert-led brands where content is knowledge and insight driven

- Experience creating content for service-based businesses or B2B audiences

- A strong eye for what performs in short-form video

- Experience growing a YouTube channel from a small base

- Experience with cross-platform content repurposing strategies



Role details



- Part-time remote role

- Philippines-based

- Starting at approximately 10 to 20 hours per week

- Scope to grow over time if the fit is strong and the role expands into a full time role

- Ongoing role for the right person

- You will work directly with the founders



To apply

Please do the following:



Complete this Google Form, following all instructions and answering every question.







Include the words 'Grow and Scale' at the top of your application so we know you've read this description and can follow instructions

title: Construction Estimator
description:
Job Title: Lead Construction Estimator (Founding Tea
mber - 1 Spot Left) – $10/hr + Full Time



The Opportunity: Complete Our Founding Team

Are you tired of working for disorganized agencies or micromanagers who don't understand the complexities of construction estimating?



Newly Renovated Inc. is a premium U.S.-based General Contractor (San Francisco, CA) focused on residential and commercial construction. We are currently building an elite, two-person foundational proposal team based in the Philippines. We have already hired the first member of this team, and we are now looking for the second and final Lead Construction Estimator to complete the unit.



You will work directly alongside our Founder & CEO to build out our estimating systems, establish Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), and eventually help us recruit, train, and manage future estimators as we scale. If you want autonomy, respect, and a long-term career track with a forward-thinking company, this is the exact role you've been looking for.



What You Will Actually Do

• End-to-End Proposal Ownership: You are responsible for the entire proposal lifecycle. You will receive the base project information (site photos, notes, and plans), parse out all appropriate scopes of work, accurately calculate all costs and values, and deliver a 100% client-ready proposal.

• AI-Augmented Execution: We operate at the cutting edge. We expect our founding team to be highly trained in utilizing Artificial Intelligence (such as ChatGPT, Claude, etc.) as a supplemental tool. You will leverage AI to draft compelling proposal narratives, refine scopes of work, and enhance your overall capabilities as an estimator and a strategic tea
mber.

• Scope Assembly & RFI Generation: Analyze incoming field data to identify hidden cost drivers, flag missing information, and proactively formulate intelligent RFI (Requests for Information) questions for the U.S. field team before the estimate is finalized.

• System Building & Leadership: Work directly with the Founder & CEO to document these AI-driven workflows, build Standard • Operating Procedures (SOPs), and eventually onboard, train, and manage future estimators as the company scales.



What We Are Looking For (Must-Haves)

• 2+ Years U.S. Estimating Experience: You must have a track record of remote estimating for U.S.-based residential or commercial construction companies.

• Principles Over Platforms: We don't demand proficiency in one specific software on day one. Whether you use Buildertrend, JobTread, Procore, or PlanSwift, we care that you fundamentally understand the mechanics of construction pricing, takeoffs, and scopes. We will train you on our specific stack.

• English Proficiency: B1 (Intermediate) minimum required, though C1/C2 (Advanced/Fluent) is highly expected. You must be able to write clear, professional scopes of work and communicate effectively on video calls.

• Tech Setup: A modern PC/Laptop and highly reliable, fast internet capable of handling video calls and cloud-based software without lag.



The Offer & Schedule

• Role: Full-Time, Independent Contractor (100% Remote)

• Compensation: $10.00 USD per hour (Premium market rate)

• Schedule: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM Pacific Standard Time (PST), Monday – Friday.

• Payments: Processed securely and reliably via Deel.



How to Apply & The Interview Process (READ CAREFULLY)

We are looking for highly attentive professionals. Do not
 us directly, and do not send generic messages on this platform. 1. Submit the Application: Fill out our official application form here: 

2. Attention to Detail Check: Inside the Google Form, there is a field that asks for your "Title/Role." Please type the exact phrase "Founding Builder" in that box so we know you read this entire description.

3. Video Introduction: Be prepared to provide a link to a 1–3 minute video introducing yourself, explaining your end-to-end estimating experience, and sharing why you want to be the final addition to this founding team.

4. The Founder Interview: Upon successful review of your complete application (including the secret phrase and intro video), you will be invited to a video interview directly with our Founder & CEO. This interview is a key component of the job offer process, where we will discuss your technical approach to estimating, your ability to leverage AI, and ensure a strong cultural fit for this founding role.



Applications missing the secret phrase or the intro video will be automatically deleted.

title: Short-Form Video Editor for Fitness Influencer
description:
Role Description

We are looking for a short-form video editor to help turn raw fitness, lifestyle, and talking-head footage into polished, on-brand content for Instagram and TikTok. This person’s main job is to reduce the founder’s editing workload, improve content consistency, and help turn one filming session into multiple usable pieces of content.



Roles and responsibilities

 • Edit short-form vertical videos for Instagram Reels and TikTok

 • Cut workout footage, talking-head clips, voiceovers, and b-roll into engaging content

 • Add captions, text overlays, punch-ins, pacing improvements, and light effects where appropriate

 • Follow the brand’s aesthetic and editing style consistently

 • Create multiple cutdowns or alternate versions from the same source footage

 • Organize raw footage and final assets in a clean folder structure for future use cases

 • Export platform-ready files in the correct format and quality

 • Raw footage gets turned into polished, publish-ready content quickly and consistently (3-5 posts/week)

What this role is not responsible for

 • Being the face or voice of the brand

 • Creating overall business strategy

 • Writing sales copy or managing leads

title: Shopify Web Developer - CRO Specialist - Ecommerce Advertising Agency - Full Time 4b
description:
APPLY ON MY WEBSITE TO BE CONSIDERED: 





CRO Shopify Web Developer – Job Overview

I am looking for an experienced shopify web developer to help me upgrade client’s checkout processes and execute CRO tests for various ecommerce brands.

In this role you will update Shopify theme templates, develop landing pages using various page builders and html/CSS frameworks, research/install various checkout/bundle applications, and implement A/B tests using testing tools like intelligems.

You will also report on test performance, and provide suggestions for next steps.

This is a full-time opportunity. Training will be provided and you will work directly with me.



About Zack Miller

My name is Zack Miller, and I’ve been a DTC Ecommerce Growth Marketer since 2019. I’ve done everything fro
dia buying and client strategy, to agency internal operations.

I’ve contributed to the growth of over 200 ecommerce brands in my agency experience. Now, as an independent growth marketing partner, I work closely with a small number of brands on their new customer acquisition strategy.



Note: All Remote Employees Must Be Available To Work During The Following Business Hours:

Monday: 9am – 5:30pm EST

Tuesday: 9am – 5:30pm EST

Wednesday: 9am – 5:30pm EST

Thursday: 9am – 5:30pm EST

Friday: 9am – 5:30pm EST



Roles & Responsibilities

- Develop landing pages using Figma and various WYSYWIG editors including but not limited to Replo and Pagefly.

- Edit product, collection and other page templates using Shopify Liquid template engine, and various HTML/CSS frameworks.

- Install various checkout/tracking applications (aftersell, Zipify OCU, Upstack, etc)

- Install various applications for product bundling when needed, and create product bundles. 

- Install tracking pixels on client’s websites

- Implement A/B tests in intelligems for product price tests, shipping price tests, theme tests, landing page tests, and more. 

- Research new ideas for direct response content marketing strategies.

- Work in my project management system, ClickUp.

- Apply a process-first mentality and provide feedback to help further develop our operating systems.



Required Experience & Education

- Strong verbal and written English language skills (American Dialect)

- Strong knowledge of HTML/CSS/Javascript/Shopify Liquid required. 

- Knowledge of Python and Ruby preferred but not required.

- 1+ years of web development (Shopify) experience required

- 1+ years of backend python (flask/django) development experience preferred but not required

- 1+ years of marketing agency experience preferred but not required

- Must be highly organized and possess strong attention to detail

- All applicants are encouraged to apply, regardless of experience or education

• Strong written English communication

***Compensation and Growth***

Career path toward Senior Sourcer or full-cycle Recruiter roles

title: Facebook/Meta Ads Media Buyer — DTC E-Commerce (Ongoing)
description:
We're a fast-growing DTC e-commerce company looking for someone who knows Meta Ads Manager inside and out to handle the daily uploading and setup of our
 ad campaigns.

Important: This is NOT a media buying or strategy role. You won't be making optimization decisions or managing budgets. You will be uploading ad creatives into our accounts following our exact structure, naming conventions, and settings — with zero room for shortcuts or assumptions.

The Role:

You'll be responsible for correctly uploading new ad creatives into our Meta ad accounts on a daily basis. Every ad must be uploaded manually through Ads Manager — no third-party tools, no bulk uploaders, no automation software of any kind.

The workload is roughly 1 hour per day right now but will increase over time. You must be available 7 days a week.

What You'll Be Doing:



Manually uploading new ad creatives into Meta Ads Manager daily

Following our exact campaign structure: 1 CBO ? 1 ad set containing 3 ads. Each new creative brief = a new ad set with 3 new ads

Applying our specific naming template to every campaign, ad set, and ad — no exceptions

Ensuring all settings are correct before publishing: proper Page selection, correct identity, placements, etc.

Turning OFF all auto-enhancements (Advantage+ creative, text enhancements, etc.) unless told otherwise

Double-checking every detail before anything goes live



What We're Looking For:



Deep working knowledge of Meta Ads Manager — you know where every setting lives and what it does

Experience with manual ad uploading at volume

Extremely detail-oriented and process-driven

Understands CBO campaign structures

Knows how to disable auto-enhancements and why it matters

Comfortable following strict SOPs and naming conventions without deviation

Reliable, consistent, available 7 days/week including weekends

No reliance on third-party ad management tools — everything is done natively in Ads Manager



Details:



Remote, ongoing contract

~1 hr/day to start, growing over time

7 days/week availability required

$8–$15/hr depending on experience



To Apply:

Tell us your experience with Meta Ads Manager. Specifically: how many accounts have you worked in, do you know how to disable Advantage+ auto-enhancements, and are you comfortable following a strict naming convention and campaign structure with no deviations? Bonus if you can share screenshots of accounts you've worked in.

Ji Martial Arts is a practitioner-led martial arts education and performance brand founded by brothers Joshua and Joel Iyalla — elite martial artists with over 35 years of combined experience across multiple disciplines, international competition, and world record performance.



We are not a marketing company that chose martial arts. We are martial artists who built a brand.



The business operates across content creation, performance equipment, and education — with a combined audience of over 3 million across Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, and
. Our flagship product is the Ji Full Body Resistance Band, with further products and programmes in development.



Everything we build is content-first, practitioner-led, and rooted in discipline, accountability, and proof. Our mission is to make martial arts more accessible — especially for young people who need real role models.



This is a fast-moving, high-standard environment. We do not cut corners and we do not operate on excuses.





THE ROLE:

We are looking for a highly capable Operations Manager / Executive Assistant to work directly with the founders of Ji Martial Arts as we scale.



This is not a traditional VA or admin role.



You will act as a right-hand operator — helping manage day-to-day operations, content workflows,
 ads production, YouTube pipelines, influencer and ambassador programmes, supplier communications, and finance administration across the business.



This role suits someone who is organised, proactive, detail-obsessed, and able to take full ownership of tasks without constant hand-holding. You will be working alongside a small, experienced team and will be responsible for making sure things get done — not just coordinating them.





KEY RESPONSIBILITY EXAMPLES:

The following are examples of the type of work this role involves — this list is not exhaustive. As the business scales, the scope will grow and evolve. If you need a fixed job description to feel comfortable, this is not the right role for you.



- Rebuild the Ji Notion workspace from the ground up and manage it as the central operating hub for the entire business

- Own the full YouTube long-form production pipeline — from generating and getting ideas approved, to overseeing edits, building in CapCut, creating thumbnails, and scheduling

- 
 ad performance data, build new ad creatives end to end, and draft campaigns in full so the founders only need to approve and launch

- Research, outreach, and onboard martial arts influencers and brand ambassadors — managing the full relationship and holding them accountable to content targets

- Coordinate the team day to day — setting weekly call agendas, taking live action items, chasing deliverables, and quality-checking work before it reaches the founders

- Build and manage a content reposting system in Airtable — tracking performance across all platforms and flagging videos eligible for reposting based on data

- Act as the primary point of contact for all manufacturer and supplier communications — handling negotiations and order management end to end





WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR:

- Strong written and spoken English

- High level of organisation and attention to detail

- Comfortable working across tools including Notion, Airtable, Google Drive, and social platforms

- Able to take initiative and make decisions without waiting to be told

- Experience managing or coordinating a team

- Comfortable handling financial admin and supplier communications

- Background in operations, marketing, media, or executive support is a strong plus

- Genuine interest in martial arts, fitness, or performance culture is a plus





IMPORTANT:

This is not a basic admin or data-entry role.



Ji Martial Arts is built on high standards, discipline, and proof. We expect the same from the people we work with. If you need step-by-step instructions for every task, this role is not for you.



We want someone who sees a problem and solves it. Someone who takes ownership, communicates clearly, and gets things over the line.



HOW TO APPLY:

To apply, please complete the short application form here: 



Applications submitted without completing the form will not be considered.



Skill Requirements: Operations Management · Executive Assistance · Content & Social Media · Financial Administration ·
 Marketing

title: Claims File Reviewer / Auditor Location
description:
# Claims File Reviewer / Auditor



## About This Role



We are a licensed public adjusting firm and roofing company based in Chicagoland, Illinois, and indiana,  that represents homeowners against insurance companies. When a homeowner has property damage (hail, wind, ), the insurance company sends their own adjuster to write an estimate. Our job is to review that estimate, find everything the carrier missed or underpaid, and fight to get the insured the full amount they are owed under their policy.



The File Reviewer is the person who keeps every active claim file moving forward and makes sure nothing falls through the cracks. You will review insurance policies, audit damage estimates, compare photos against scopes of work, draft follow-up
s to insurance carriers looking for policy's and missed scopes, and prepare damage summaries photo reports, that support our negotiations.



You will be trained on insurance terminology, property damage identification, and claim file strategy. You need to be sharp, detail-oriented, and comfortable learning a technical subject matter.



---



## What You Will Do Every Day



### 1. Policy Review & Coverage Analysis



You will open the insured's insurance policy and pull out the information that matters for the claim. This includes:



- Coverage limits (how much the policy will pay for dwelling, other structures, personal property, loss of use)

- Whether the policy pays Replacement Cost or Actual Cash Value

- Endorsements that increase coverage (extended replacement cost, ordinance or law, water backup)

- Sublimits or exclusions the carrier might use to reduce or deny parts of the claim

- Confirm the date of loss falls within the policy period

- Identify the deductible amount and type (flat dollar vs. percentage-based wind/hail deductible)



**Example:** A homeowner's HO-3 policy shows Coverage A (Dwelling) at $350,000 with a replacement cost provision and a 25% extended replacement cost endorsement. You confirm this means up to $437,500 is available for dwelling repairs. You also flag an ordinance or law endorsement that covers code upgrade costs, which the carrier has not addressed in their estimate.



---



### 2. File Auditing: Scope Comparison & Missed Damage Identification



This is the most critical part of the role. You will compare the insurance carrier's estimate against our internal scope and the inspection photos, line by line, looking for anything the carrier missed, excluded, or underpriced.



**What you are looking for:**



**Roof:**

- Carrier replaces the main roof field but omits ridge cap shingles or ridge vent replacement

- Missing starter strip, drip edge, ice and water shield, or other code-required items

- Pipe boots, step flashing, counter flashing, and chimney flashing visible as damaged in photos but not included in the carrier's scope

- Ventilation upgrades required by current building code but excluded from the estimate



**Siding:**

- Carrier scopes partial siding repair on one elevation, but photos show hail impact damage across multiple elevations

- Vinyl or aluminum siding where a full elevation or full house replacement is required to achieve a uniform appearance and restore the property in a workmanlike manner

- Carrier prices "patch repair" when the damage pattern across multiple elevations requires full replacement



**Window Wraps / Capping:**

- Aluminum window and door trim wraps showing denting, creasing, or paint chipping from hail

- Carriers routinely exclude these entirely or price them as "touch-up paint" instead of full re-wrap

- Check every window and door on every elevation against the photos



**Gutters & Downspouts:**

- Denting and bruising visible in photos but carrier only included one or two sections

- Check all elevations for gutter damage

- Confirm downspouts, elbows, splash blocks, and gutter screens are all accounted for



**Soffit & Fascia:**

- Hail or wind damage to aluminum soffit panels and fascia wrap

- These are frequently visible in photos but completely missing from the carrier's scope



**Paint / Stain:**

- Interior or exterior surfaces that need repaint after repairs

- Carrier may include repair of a ceiling or wall but exclude the paint needed to finish the job

- Exterior surfaces requiring full repaint to maintain a uniform appearance after partial replacement



**Interior Damages:**

- Water stains on ceilings or walls visible in photos that indicate water intrusion from roof damage

- No interior line items in the carrier's scope despite visible evidence of interior water damage

- Check kitchen, bathroom, bedroom, and hallway ceiling/wall photos for staining or water marks



**Overhead & Profit (O&P):**

- When three or more trades are involved in the repair (roofing, siding, gutters, interior, paint, etc.), general contractor overhead and profit should be included

- Flag every file where the scope involves multiple trades but O&P has been excluded



**Code Upgrades:**

- Drip edge, ice and water shield, starter strip, ventilation, and other items required by current building code that the carrier has not included

- These are owed under ordinance or law coverage and are frequently left out of carrier estimates



**Example:** The carrier's Xactimate estimate includes roof replacement on two slopes and one elevation of siding. Inspection photos show hail impacts on siding across three additional elevations, dented window wraps on eight windows, crushed gutter sections on the north and west elevations, and a water stain on the master bedroom ceiling. None of these appear in the carrier's scope. You flag each missed item with the corresponding photo reference and prepare a list for the supplement demand.



---



### 3. Damage Report Preparation



You will review inspection photos and field notes to prepare written damage summaries. These summaries describe what damage is present, what caused it, and what repairs are needed to restore the property to its pre-loss condition.



These reports are used to support supplement requests, appraisal demands, and carrier negotiations. They need to be clear, specific, and professional.



**Example:** Field photos show granule displacement and fracturing on three-tab shingles across all roof slopes, bruising on aluminum gutters, and dented drip edge. You prepare a summary documenting each damaged component, the number of affected slopes, the hail impact pattern, and why a full roof replacement (not spot repair) is required to restore the roof in a workmanlike manner.



---



### 4. Outbound Correspondence & Follow-Up



You will draft and send status
s to insurance carriers requesting:



- Claim status updates

- Adjuster assignments and inspection scheduling

- Estimate releases and coverage determinations

- Payment status and check issuance

- Responses to our supplement requests



You will track response timelines and flag files where carriers have gone silent or are approaching regulatory response deadlines. No file should sit idle without a follow-up on the calendar.



**Example:** A State Farm field adjuster inspected a property three weeks ago but no estimate has been released. You send a follow-up
 referencing the inspection date, requesting the estimate and any coverage determination, and noting the elapsed time since inspection.



---



### 5. CRM Documentation



You will log all file activity in the CRM with clear notes. Notes should summarize what was reviewed, what was found, and what action is needed next. Every note tags the admin team so nothing gets lost.



---



## Tools You Will Use



- **CRM / Claims Management System** (you will be trained on our system)

- **Xactimate** or **Xactimate Reports** (insurance estimating software; you will learn to read and compare these)

- **Google Workspace** (Gmail, Drive, Docs, Sheets)

- **PDF Viewers** for policies, inspection reports, and carrier correspondence

- **Photo Management** for reviewing property damage documentation



---



## This Role Is a Good Fit If You...



- Are extremely detail-oriented; you catch things other people miss

- Can learn a new industry quickly; you will be trained on insurance claims, but you need to absorb and retain technical information

- Are comfortable working independently and managing a queue of 20 to 50+ active files

- Can write clear, professional
s in English

- Are organized and proactive; you follow up without being told to follow up

- Have experience in insurance, claims, restoration, or construction (a strong plus, but not required if you are a fast learner)

- Can compare two documents side by side and spot the differences

- Are comfortable reviewing property damage photos (roofs, siding, gutters, interiors) and learning to identify damage types



---



## What You Will NOT Be Doing



- You will NOT be making phone calls (this is an
 and documentation role)

- You will NOT be in the field inspecting properties

- You will NOT be writing Xactimate estimates from scratch (you are reviewing and comparing existing estimates)

- You will NOT be providing legal advice or making strategic claim decisions (that is the principal's role)



---



## Schedule & Compensation



- Full-time position (40 hours per week)

- Compensation commensurate with experience

- Paid via Wise

- Must be available during US business hours (Central Time) for overlap and communication

- Long-term position with growth potential as you develop insurance expertise

- Speaks and writes in english



---



## How to Apply



Send a message that includes:



1. A brief summary of your relevant experience (insurance, claims, admin, document review, or similar detail-oriented roles)

2. Describe a time you caught an error or found something that was missed in a document, report, or process

3. Confirm your availability and that you can work during US Central Time business hours

title: E-Commerce Customer Support Representative (Phone & Chat)
description:
About MIRA Safety

MIRA Safety is committed to protecting lives through high-performance safety equipment trusted by civilians, professionals, and first responders worldwide. Our mission is to deliver reliable solutions that help customers stay prepared for the unexpected.

As we continue to grow, we are expanding our support team and seeking experienced professionals who thrive in e-commerce and product-driven support environments, supporting U.S.-based customers.



Role Overview

We are hiring an E-Commerce Customer Support Representative with strong BPO voice experience and proven ability to handle both phone and live chat support simultaneously.

This is not a purely transactional support role. We are looking for individuals who can confidently engage customers, communicate product value, and take full ownership of resolving issues in real time.



This role is ideal for someone who:

-Has experience in e-commerce or product-based support

-Can effectively manage phone and live chat concurrently

-Demonstrates high energy, adaptability, and ownership

-Is confident explaining products and guiding customer decisions

-Accurately document all interactions in CRM systems

Meet or exceed performance metrics, including:

-Collaborate with fulfillment and sales teams when necessary

Required Qualifications

-Minimum 3+ years of BPO voice support experience with U.S. customers

-Proven experience handling high-volume support across phone and chat simultaneously

-Strong spoken English with a neutral accent

    Gorgias

    Zendesk

Work Requirements

-Consistent availability during scheduled shifts



Additional technical requirements will be provided during the hiring process.



Schedule

Monday to Friday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM CST. Schedule may adjust based on business needs

-Strong product knowledge and ability to recommend solutions confidently

T-rack record of high engagement, responsiveness, and ownership in customer interactions

title: Social Media Content & Community Coordinator
description:
Great choice! Here's the fully updated posting:



---



**Social Media Content & Community Coordinator | Part-Time | Remote | Contract**



We're a social media marketing agency looking for a creative and driven Social Media Content & Community Coordinator to join our team. In this role, you'll focus on producing scroll-stopping graphics and videos that bring our clients' brands to life across social media platforms — while also playing an active role in scheduling, publishing, and community engagement. If you have a strong eye for design, a passion for video, and love collaborating with a tight-knit team, we'd love to hear from you.

*Content Planning & Publishing*

- Assist with coordinating content approvals between the team and clients



*Community & Engagement*

**What We're Looking For**

title: Remote Residential Roofing Estimator / Project Coordinator (Part-Time, Growing to Full-Time)
description:
About the Role

We are a fast-growing residential roofing company based in the United States currently processing 15–40 roofing jobs per month — and we are scaling. We are looking for a high-performing Remote Roofing Estimator and Project Coordinator who wants to grow with us. This role starts part-time but is designed to transition into a full-time position with increased pay as our volume grows. We're not looking for someone to fill a seat — we're looking for someone who takes ownership, hits deadlines, and becomes an integral part of our team.

If you're a high performer who wants a long-term home with a company on the rise, this is the role for you.



What You'll Do



Measure residential roofs and build all estimates inside Roofr

Manage jobs and track project status inside Roofr CRM

Deliver completed estimates within 12 hours of inspection — this is a firm requirement

Prepare work orders for field crews based on approved estimates

Build accurate material lists for each job

Communicate directly with the owner on job details, scope, and priorities





Requirements — Please Read Before Applying





Solid understanding of residential roofing components: shingles, underlayment, drip edge, ice & water shield, ridge cap, starter strip, etc.

Familiarity with US roofing suppliers, specifically ABC Supply and QXO

Able to consistently hit a 12-hour turnaround from inspection to completed estimate

Can independently manage 15–40 estimates and jobs per month

Strong written English communication

Reliable high-speed internet and dedicated workspace

A self-starter who takes pride in accuracy and reliability





Nice to Have



Prior remote work with a US residential roofing contractor

Experience building crew work orders and material lists for field teams





Why Join Us



This role grows with you — as our company scales, this position moves to full-time with higher pay

Work directly with the owner — no middlemen, no corporate layers

Be a key part of a growing roofing operation, not just a task worker

Flexible hours — work when it works for you as long as deadlines are met

Consistent, reliable work every month

Paid weekly via Wise

We invest in high performers — raises tied to performance and volume growth





To Apply

Send your resume along with a short paragraph covering:



Your hands-on experience with Roofr (estimating + CRM)

Types of residential roofing jobs you've estimated

Whether you've prepped work orders and material lists before

Your ability to consistently meet a 12-hour estimate turnaround

mbers in the department regarding scheduling,

title: Paid Consultation — Current Meta / Google / Ad Platform Employees
description:
We're a DTC brand spending significant budget on Meta and scaling into other paid channels. We're looking for someone who currently works at Meta, Google, TikTok, or any major ad platform in an ads-related role for ongoing paid consultations.



Roles we're interested in:



Meta Pro / Meta Business Support / Client Solutions



Google Ads Support / Google Customer Solutions



TikTok Ads support or partnership roles



Any ad platform employee on any platform in account management, ad review, policy, partnerships, or technical support



What we need from you:



Help us understand how the platform actually works from the inside — account structure, policy enforcement, best practices that aren't in the public docs



Guidance on ad account health, avoiding restrictions, and scaling spend safely



Insight into review processes, flagging systems, and how to stay compliant (especially in the supplements/health space)



Someone we can message when something breaks or gets flagged and get a real answer, not a bot response

We also pay referral fees. If you know someone at Meta, Google, TikTok, or any ad platform who'd be open to this, connect us and we'll pay you for the intro.



To apply: Tell us where you work, what your role is, and what you can help with. Keep it short.

title: Creative Operations Specialist
description:
We’re looking for a highly capable Creative Operations Specialist who can take creative initiatives and drive projects independently. This role is perfect for someone who thrives on challenges, is confident in their skills, and is ready to work at a high standard — while also contributing to structured workflows and systems.



What You’ll Do:

• Edit and produce short-form videos, reels, and other content with professional-level quality, captions, and consistent branding.

• Turn AI outputs into brand-aligned video and written content.

• Support social media growth and content execution.

• Build and improve structured systems and SOPs.

• Organize digital files and manage multiple projects efficiently.



What We’re Looking For:

• Confident self-starter who can take creative challenges and handle constructive feedback.

• Strong video editing skills with a good eye for aesthetics and branding.

• Expert-level knowledge of AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Higgsfield, etc.) and ability to use them for problem-solving and content creation.

• Strong organizational skills, systems thinking, and ability to independently manage projects from start to finish.

• Comfortable providing timely updates and proactively pushing initiatives forward, even if not perfect.

• Access to a reliable Apple device



Preferred:

• Experience in digital operations, real estate, sales, or service-based businesses.

• Some design sense and understanding of branding is a plus.



Details:

• Full-Time, Mon–Fri, 8 AM–5 PM EST

• $800 USD/month ($5/hour)

• 13th-Month Pay

• Paid Time Off

• Weekends Off

• Benefits allowance after 6 months



To Apply:

• Send your resume and portfolio

• Screenshot showing your computer specs and internet speed.

• Answer: “Why are you a good fit for this role?”

title: Direct Response Video Ad Scriptwriter
description:
We're a performance marketing team looking for a dedicated scriptwriter, not a virtual assistant, who understands why people buy, not just someone who writes grammatically correct English. Our video ads (Meta and YouTube, 30 seconds to 3 minutes) use psychological persuasion frameworks like PAS, FOMO, social proof, urgency, and loss aversion. If those terms mean something to you, keep reading.



What you'll actually do:



Your core job is writing video ad scripts that stop the scroll and drive conversions. This means:

* Writing 2–3 short-form video ad scripts per day using proven direct response frameworks (we'll provide training, templates, and a swipe file of our winning scripts)

* Creating 3–5 hook variations per script — the first 3 seconds determine whether the ad works or fails

* Rewriting and iterating on existing scripts based on performance data (you'll see what's working and why)

* Researching hooks, angles, and competitor ads using Meta Ad Library, TikTok Creative Center, and VidTao, as well as other Social medias.

* As you grow: voicing scripts with AI tools, briefing our video editor in ClickUp, and contributing original creative concepts



What we need from you:

* You understand persuasion psychology, not just writing. You know the difference between features and benefits. You can explain why loss aversion works differently than social proof.

* Strong conversational English writing that sounds like a real person talking to a friend, not a brochure.

* You can write WITHOUT AI generating your scripts. (AI as a brainstorming tool is fine. Copy-pasting ChatGPT output is not. We will test for this.) When you reach out to apply, include the word "hook" in the title of your message so we know you read the full posting.

* Available to work 3 hours/day, 5 days/week (Monday–Friday). Preferred hours: 10 AM–1 PM EST / 10 PM–1 AM Manila time, with flexibility once you're ramped up.

* Familiar with or willing to learn: ChatGPT or other AI tools (as a research aid), ClickUp (task management),
 (daily communication).



Compensation:

* $5–$7/hour to start based on experience

* Performance bonuses when your scripts scale

* Clear path to higher rate and more hours as you prove your ability

* Paid monthly via PayPal



How to apply:

Send me a message here with a brief introduction and I will send you a link to our application form.

The form includes a few questions about your background and a short writing exercise to see how you think and write under real conditions. We just want to see how you approach persuasion and creative problem-solving in your own words.

We review applications on a rolling basis over the course of a week to give more candidates an opportunity to apply. 



About us:

We're a performance marketing team. We value skill and initiative over credentials. If you deliver scripts that convert, you'll be rewarded with raises, bonuses, and more responsibility. We've designed a structured onboarding process with training materials, a script library, and clear frameworks so you can ramp up quickly.

title: Senior Marketing Operations Specialist
description:
Job Description

Intelligent Technical Solutions (ITS) is seeking a highly technical, detail-oriented Senior Marketing Operations Specialist to own and optimize our marketing automation infrastructure. This role is responsible for building, maintaining, and improving HubSpot systems that drive lead generation, nurturing, segmentation, and revenue attribution.



This individual will serve as the marketing team’s HubSpot and integration expert — ensuring seamless connectivity between HubSpot, ZoomInfo, sales systems, and other platforms. The ideal candidate is analytical, systems-driven, process-minded, and passionate about automation, data integrity, and measurable marketing performance. 



Job Responsibilities

HubSpot Management & Optimization 



Own and maintain HubSpot Marketing Hub and CRM architecture.

Build and optimize lists, workflows, sequences, forms, and landing pages.

Create and manage
 campaigns, nurture sequences, and automation flows.

Implement lead scoring models and lifecycle stage management.

Ensure CRM hygiene, deduplication, and segmentation best practices 

Integrations & Technical Operations 



Manage HubSpot integrations (ZoomInfo, CallRail, ConnectWise, etc.).

Troubleshoot sync issues between marketing and sales systems.

Oversee data imports, exports, and enrichment processes.

Evaluate and recommend new tools or automation improvements 

Campaign Execution Support 



Build campaign infrastructure inside HubSpot (lists, workflows, triggers).

Create segmentation strategies for targeted outreach.

Support ABM and ZoomInfo-driven outbound efforts.

Conduct A/B testing and performance optimization 

Reporting & Analytics 



Build dashboards for marketing KPIs and attribution.

Track MQL, SQL, pipeline, and revenue influence.

Monitor campaign performance and recommend improvements.

Present monthly marketing performance reports 

Process Documentation & Training 



Document HubSpot processes and SOPs.

Train tea
mbers on automation and CRM best practices.

Maintain compliance with internal data governance standards 



Job Qualifications

5–8+ years of marketing operations experience

Advanced proficiency in HubSpot Marketing Hub & CRM (required).

Experience integrating HubSpot with ZoomInfo and other sales tools.

Strong understanding of
 marketing, automation logic, and segmentation.

Experience building workflows, sequences, and attribution models.

Working knowledge of APIs and integration tools (Zapier, native integrations, etc.).

Strong analytical and reporting skills.

Highly detail-oriented and process-driven.

HubSpot certifications strongly preferred 



Job KPIs

CRM data accuracy > 95%

Workflow error rate < 3?mpaign launch timelines met 100%

MQL to SQL conversion rate improvement (quarter-over-quarter)

Attribution reporting accuracy and timeliness

 performance benchmarks (open rate, CTR, conversion rate) 



Job Characteristics

Systems thinker

Analytical and data-driven 

Detail-oriented 

Process builder 

Proactive problem solver 

Strong cross-functional communicator 



Job Requirements

A home office set up that meets the following:

Windows 11 with an Intel i5 or i7 (8th generation or newer), or

AMD Ryzen 5 or 7 (2000 series or newer), or

macOS 12 or newer.

Computer: A working computer with a minimum of 8GB RAM and one of the following:

Internet: Stable internet connection with at least 5 Mbps download and upload speeds.

Audio Equipment: A quality headset with a noise-cancellation feature for meetings.

Mobile Device: A smartphone capable of installing the Microsoft Authenticator app.

Webcam: An HD webcam for video calls.

A very strong cultural fit the ITS way (Our core values will be discussed during the interview)

Excellent command of the English language both spoken and written

Able to work full time 40 hrs. a week: 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM PST (Monday to Friday)

Able to pass our online behavior and cognitive assessments.



Compensation

Pay rate starts at Php66,600.00 and may vary by experience and location.



Benefits

Comprehensive Health Plans / HMO (Employee & Dependent/s)

Life Insurance

Flexible Benefit Reimbursement (Dental/Vision/Mental Health/Wellness/Government Contributions)

Paid Time Off (starts at 15 days per year)

Maternity/Paternity Leave

Paid US Holiday

Night Shift Bonus

Salary Advancement/Loan

Health & Wellness Program

Company-paid training and certification

title: Leasing Manager (LTR & PadSplit)
description:
Are you great at selling tenants on rentals, screening them fast, and closing deals all the way through lease signing and move-in?



Can you start adding value on day one, taking ownership of listings, leads, and conversions?



We’re a fast-growing property management group managing LTRs and PadSplit homes. We’re looking for someone smart, diligent, and proactive — someone who thrives on results, not supervision.



If you know how to:

- Market and lease properties quickly

- Prequalify and screen tenants effectively

- Coordinate move-ins and manage lead funnels

…then you’ll fit right in.





Long term, you’ll have the chance to build and lead your own leasing team as we scale.





Requirements:

- Strong leasing or property management experience (LTR or co-living preferred)

- Excellent spoken and written English (C2 level)

- High attention to detail, strong follow-through, and responsive communication

- Available to work U.S. EST/ET hours





If this sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now and start making an impact immediately.





DO NOT APPLY IF:



Your English is not near-native or highly fluent (C2 level).

You do not have a strong leasing background.

You do not have a background in sales.

You are not tech-savvy.

You are not detail-oriented and highly diligent.

You need constant supervision or struggle to take ownership.





Application Process:

- Submit your resume and a short video introduction (via Google Drive or Loom — make sure the link is accessible). In your video, briefly introduce yourself and share why you’d be great for this role.

- Send us a message on OnlineJobs.ph explaining why you’re the right fit and what makes you stand out.

- Wait for our response — we carefully review every application and will contact shortlisted candidates for the next steps.



Thank you!

title: Marketing Project Manager
description:
Job Description:

Intelligent Technical Solutions (ITS) is seeking a highly organized and proactive Project Manager to ensure marketing initiatives and cross-departmental projects are delivered on time and without bottlenecks. This role will live inside Notion, owning project visibility, documentation, and the creation of SOPs and operational documents that improve clarity, accountability, and scalability across departments.  



This role acts as the accountability engine of the marketing department — tracking deadlines, securing approvals, coordinating SMEs, and ensuring no task falls through the cracks. The ideal candidate thrives in fast-paced environments, communicates clearly, follows up consistently, and ensures projects move forward efficiently. 



Job Responsibilities:

Project Oversight & Deadline Management 

Own marketing project timelines from kickoff to completion 

Maintain master marketing calendar and campaign roadmap 

Track deliverables across marketing, sales, leadership, and product teams 

Ensure no tasks, approvals, or deadlines are missed 

Cross-Department Coordination 

Follow up with SMEs for content interviews and approvals 

Coordinate quote approvals, product approvals, and leadership reviews 

Ensure timely feedback loops between departments 

Schedule and manage recurring project meetings 

Task & Workflow Management 

Maintain project boards (Notion, Strety, etc.) 

Assign tasks and track completion 

Identify bottlenecks and escalate when necessary 

Improve workflow efficiency and documentation 

Meeting & Communication Management 

Run status meetings and distribute action items 

Provide weekly project status updates 

Maintain documentation of processes and project SOPs 

Quality Control & Accountability 

Ensure all marketing materials move through proper approval workflows 

Confirm deliverables are finalized before launch 

Monitor alignment between marketing strategy and execution 



Job Qualifications:

3–6+ years of project management experience 

Experience working in marketing or cross-functional teams 

Strong organizational and time-management skills 

Excellent follow-up and communication abilities 

Experience with project management software 

Ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously 

Strong documentation skills 



Preferred: 

PMP or project management certification 

Experience in B2B or technology environments 



Job KPIs:

100% on-time campaign launches

title: Short-Form Video Editor for Paid Social Ads
description:
Short-Form Video Editor for Paid Social Ads (DTC E-Commerce)



We are an agency producing high-converting ads primarily for DTC e-commerce brands running paid campaigns on Meta and TikTok.



Our clients span a wide range of consumer product categories, including:



• Wellness & recovery (saunas, cold plunges)

• Fashion & apparel (streetwear, boutiques, beachwear)

• Beauty & personal care (including wigs)

• Food & beverage / CPG

• Outdoor & lifestyle gear

• Hobby & gaming accessories

• Subscription boxes & membership brands

• Gardening & plants

• Workwear & safety apparel



This role is NOT for organic social editors or YouTube editors.



We are looking for a video editor experienced in creating paid ad creative for e-commerce brands. If your background is primarily organic social media editing, this role will not be the right fit.



Required Experience:

Applicants must have:

Experience editing paid ads for Meta and/or TikTok

Previous work with e-commerce / product-based brands

Portfolio examples of short-form video ads 

Understanding of direct response creative (hooks, retention, product messaging, CTA)

Expertise in generating AI product imagery and video that is both accurate and on-brand

Bonus: AI UGC generation



Expertise In: (not limited to)

Gemini Nano Banana / VEO3 (or preferred equivalent)

CapCut (or preferred video editing software)

Canva (or preferred software to edit motion graphics/GIFs)



We’re Looking For Someone Who:

Understands performance marketing, not just aesthetics

Can produce high-quality videos quickly in a fast-paced agency environment

Can create multiple ad variations and repurpose content for creative testing

Is comfortable working across many different brands and product categories

Can manage creative production for multiple brands simultaneously (often up to ~20 clients) while keeping each brand’s voice, style, and guidelines consistent

Is comfortable working from creative briefs that include hooks, copy, and inspiration, while bringing their own editing expertise to elevate the final result

Can maintain strong attention to detail and organization when switching between brands and projects



Production Expectations:

Because we operate in a creative testing environment, editors should be comfortable producing multiple ad variations per day.

Typical output ranges depending on complexity:

~ 3–6 full video ads per day (UGC edits, product demos, mashup ads, etc.)

Additional variations, GIF ads, or simple motion edits will increase output

Some projects involving AI-generated assets or heavier motion graphics may take longer



The key expectation is the ability to edit efficiently while maintaining quality and brand accuracy.



When applying, please include:

Portfolio 

Examples of paid video ads you have edited for Meta or TikTok

Please prioritize any past examples for e-commerce brands

Your favorite ad you edited and why you think it performs well



Applications without paid ad examples will not be considered.

title: TikTok Content Assistant
description:
We are a women’s fashion brand looking for a TikTok Content Assistant (Video Editor + Trend Research).



You will be given raw content (videos, clips, products), and your job is to turn them into engaging, trend-driven TikTok videos.



Responsibilities:



* Edit TikTok videos using CapCut or similar

* Add hooks, captions, and trending formats

* Stay up to date with TikTok trends

* Suggest 3–5 content ideas per week

* Post or schedule content



Requirements:



* Strong understanding of TikTok trends

* Experience editing short-form content

* Ability to make content feel natural/native to TikTok

* Good English (for captions and hooks)

* Strong sense of aesthetic (fashion-focused is a plus)



Brand Aesthetic:



We are a feminine, romantic fashion brand with a soft, elevated aesthetic.



Our content style is:



* Light, clean, and minimal

* Soft, neutral colors (creams, blush, muted tones)

* Effortless, natural, not overly edited

* Modern and trendy, but still elegant



We like content that feels:



* Relaxed and aesthetic (not loud or chaotic)

* Visually pleasing and on-brand

* Similar to clean girl / soft lifestyle fashion content



Please review our Instagram before applying:





We are looking for someone who understands our aesthetic and can create content that aligns with our brand style.





To apply:



1. Send 2–3 TikTok videos you have edited

2. Briefly explain a current TikTok trend you like

3. Include the word "SWOON" in your application

4. After reviewing our Instagram, tell us:

 What type of TikTok content you would create for our brand?





Hours:



* 5-10 hours/week

* Long-term role

title: HR Sourcing Associate (Project Based - WFH)
description:
Job Description

The Sourcing Associate is responsible for proactively identifying, attracting, and engaging top talent to build a strong candidate pipeline for current and future hiring needs. They collaborate closely with recruiters and hiring managers to support the company’s talent acquisition strategy.



Job Responsibilities

Candidate Sourcing:

Proactively source candidates using job boards, LinkedIn, social media, and other platforms.

Conduct targeted searches for hard-to-fill and specialized roles.

Utilize Boolean search strategies and other creative sourcing techniques.



Talent Pool Management:

Build and maintain a database of potential candidates for current and future hiring needs.

Organize talent pools by role type (Support, Mid-level, Specialized).

Track sourcing activity and candidate statuses in internal tools.



Initial Outreach:

Draft and send engaging outreach messages to potential candidates.

Conduct preliminary screening to verify candidate interest and availability.

Forward qualified profiles to recruiters for next steps.



Collaboration and Reporting:

Work closely with recruiters and the team lead to align sourcing efforts with hiring priorities.

Provide daily or weekly reports on sourcing activities, including candidate pipelines and outreach response rates.

Identify sourcing challenges and suggest solutions



Job Qualifications

At least 1 year of recruitment or sourcing experience, ideally in a fast-paced environment and offshore accounts.

Familiarity with Boolean searches, job boards, and ATS platforms.

Strong written communication skills for candidate outreach.

Organized, proactive, and detail-oriented.

Able to follow structured sourcing procedures and meet deadlines.

Proactive, adaptable, and goal-oriented — comfortable working independently but aligned with team goals.



Job Requirements

A home office set up that meets the following:

A working computer (minimum 8gb RAM), i5 Windows10 or BigSur for Mac

Stable internet connection (at least 5mbps download and upload)

HD webcam/camera

A quality headset with noise cancellation feature for meetings

A mobile phone that has the capacity to install the Microsoft Authentication app

Able to provide NBI Clearance (for PH applicants).



Compensation

Pay rate starts at Php 49,950.00 and may vary by experience and location.



Employment Terms

Project-based for 6 months — renewable depending on hiring needs and performance.

Work hours: Flexible but aligned with core team hours (1pm - 5pm Pacific time) to ensure collaboration. Not exceeding 40 hours per week.

Semi-monthly, with clear deliverables tied to their output (e.g., number of qualified candidates sourced weekly).

No regular employee benefits.

title: Ecommerce & Marketplace Graphic Designer
description:
About the Role



We are looking for a highly creative and detail-oriented Ecommerce & Marketplace Graphic Designer to elevate our brand presence and conversion performance across digital channels. This role is not just about making things look good—it’s about driving sales through world-class visual storytelling.



You will be responsible for transforming product listings, storefronts, and digital assets into high-converting, premium experiences across platforms like Amazon, Walmart, Shopify, and beyond.



What You’ll Do



Marketplace & Ecommerce Optimization

-Design high-converting Amazon A+ Content, Walmart rich media, and marketplace listing graphics

-Create compelling product infographics that clearly communicate features, benefits, and use cases

-Develop alternative product images that improve CTR and conversion rates

-Optimize main images and gallery sequences based on platform best practices and performance data

-Optimize PDPs and marketplace listings with optimized brand look-and-feel visual guidelines



Brand Storefront & Visual Experience

-Design and maintain Amazon Storefronts and branded landing pages

-Elevate the visual consistency and storytelling of our brand across all channels

-Build cohesive campaigns that align with promotions, launches, and seasonal 
nts



Conversion-Focused Design

-Collaborate with marketing and ecommerce teams to create assets that increase conversion rate and AOV

-Translate product positioning into clear, persuasive visual messaging

-Continuously test and iterate designs based on performance data



What We’re Looking For



Core Skills

-3+ years of experience in ecommerce or marketplace graphic design

-Strong portfolio showcasing Amazon A+ Content, infographics, and product listings

-Deep understanding of conversion-driven design principles



Proficiency in:

-Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator)

-Figma

-Ability to balance branding + performance marketing

-Marketplace Knowledge (Highly Preferred)



Experience designing for:

-Amazon (A+, Brand Store, main images)

-Walmart Marketplace

-Shopify ecommerce stores

-Understanding of Amazon best practices (image compliance, hierarchy, mobile-first design)



Mindset

-Obsessed with results, not just aesthetics

-Strong attention to detail and ability to meet deadlines

-Proactive communicator who can work independently in a remote environment

-Comfortable receiving feedback and iterating quickly



What Success Looks Like

-Improved conversion rates across product listings

-Increased click-through rates from stronger main images

-Elevated brand perception across marketplaces

-Faster, more scalable production of high-quality design assets



Why Join Us

Be part of a fast-growing ecommerce company focused on building a true brand.  We want someone who is part of the team and interacting with us daily to build the Jeffers brand further with long-term growth opportunity.

title: Insurance Billing Assistant
description:
Must apply via website: 





Insurance Billing Assistant



Reports to: Account Manager





Headquarters: Savannah, GA





Job Location: Remote



What we do at Dental Claim Support:



DCS is a dental revenue cycle management company that works with dentists, dental offices, and DSOs in the United States. DCS empowers dental teams by integrating highly trained RCM experts and a unique technology framework to maximize profitability and catalyze growth. Our dental billing systems and processes allow our employees to work all aspects of the full revenue cycle system efficiently, to increase profits for dental practices and groups.



Position Summary:



Dental Claim Support is seeking a detail-oriented and organized Insurance Billing Assistant to join our team. Reporting to the Team Lead, the successful candidate will focus on managing and resolving outstanding claims on insurance aging reports and submitting daily claims, including appeals and reconsiderations. This role requires a strong understanding of dental insurance claims processes, attention to detail, and the ability to work independently to ensure claims are processed efficiently, accurately, and with all necessary supporting documentation.



How the Insurance Billing Assistant fits in:



The Insurance Billing Assistant is critical to ensuring that outstanding claims are resolved and daily claims are submitted without delay. By diligently working through insurance aging reports and managing the submission of claims, appeals, and reconsiderations, including all necessary documentation, you will directly contribute to the financial health and operational success of our clients. Collaboration with teammates is also key to this role, as you will work closely with your Team Lead and other tea
mbers to share information, resolve complex claims, and ensure consistency and accuracy in the claims process. Your ability to work effectively within a team will enhance the overall efficiency and success of the claims management process.





In this role, you will:



Managing Outstanding Claims:



Review and work through insurance aging reports to identify and resolve outstanding claims.

Follow up on pending claims with insurance carriers to ensure timely reimbursement and reduce aging accounts.



Claims Submission, Appeals, and Reconsiderations:



Prepare and submit daily claims for dental services, ensuring accuracy, compliance with payer requirements, and inclusion of all necessary supporting documentation.

Handle the submission of appeals and reconsiderations for denied or underpaid claims, providing the required documentation and follow-up.



Documentation and Data Management:



Maintain accurate and up-to-date records of claims, appeals, and reconsiderations in our database.

Ensure all claims documentation, including supporting documents, appeals, and reconsiderations, is correctly filed and accessible for audits or client inquiries.



Technical Competencies:



Demonstrate familiarity with dental insurance claim forms, coding, and payer requirements.

Utilize claims management software and databases to track the progress of claims and manage outstanding accounts.



Attention to Detail and Time Management:



Submit accurate and complete claims, appeals, and reconsiderations, adhering to deadlines and ensuring timely task completion.

Work independently to manage multiple tasks, focusing on reducing the insurance aging report and ensuring daily claims are processed with all necessary documentation.

Communicate with insurance payers, providers, and internal teams as needed, representing DCS professionally.

Assist in maintaining positive relationships with external partners to support the claims process.



Independent Work and Growth Mindset:



Manage your workload effectively, demonstrating the ability to work independently while meeting expectations.

Seek opportunities to learn and grow in the field of dental insurance claims, particularly in claims resolution, appeals, and reconsiderations.



Problem Solving and Efficiency:



Identify and resolve issues related to outstanding claims, appeals, and reconsiderations, working efficiently to minimize disruptions to client services.

Implement process improvements to enhance the accuracy and efficiency of claims processing tasks.



Client Service and Accountability:



Provide exceptional service to clients, ensuring that inquiries about outstanding claims, appeals, and reconsiderations are addressed promptly and accurately.

Take responsibility for the accuracy and completeness of claims, appeals, reconsiderations, and supporting documentation.









Success Criteria:



Client Retention and Satisfaction:



Maintain a Client Retention Rate of 90% or higher.

Maintain a Net Promoter Score (NPS) of 8 or above for accounts assigned to the Insurance Billing Assistant's specific book of business.



Revenue Generation:



Insurance Billing Assistants are expected to contribute to the individual book of business minimum equivalent to $100,000 insurance collections per month.



Outstanding Claims Resolution:



Resolve a minimum of 40 claims per day when working on outstanding claims.



Claim Submission Accuracy:



Maintain an insurance claims submission accuracy rate of 98%.



Audit Score:



Maintain an overall audit score of 90% or higher.







Requirements:



1+ year of experience in an administrative support role, preferably within a healthcare or dental setting.

Experience working as an independent contractor is preferred.

Familiarity with working with independent contractors on a global scale.

High School diploma or GED; some college coursework is a plus.

Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook, etc.); experience with credentialing software is advantageous.

Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple tasks independently.

Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal.

Ability to work independently and manage your own workload effectively.



Our Company Values:



Customer Success

Transparency

Reliability

Professional Excellence

Alignment

title: Quality Control Auditor
description:
Must apply via this application link:





Quality Control Auditor



Reports to: Quality Control Manager



Headquarters: Savannah, GA; USA





Job Location: Remote





What we do at Dental Claim Support:



DCS is a dental revenue cycle management company that works with dentists, dental offices, and DSOs in the United States. DCS empowers dental teams by integrating highly trained RCM experts and a unique technology framework to maximize profitability and catalyze growth. Our dental billing systems and processes allow our employees to work all aspects of the full revenue cycle system efficiently, to increase profits for dental practices and groups.





Position Summary:



As a Quality Control Auditor, your primary responsibility is to support the Quality Control team by performing various tasks related to auditing and verifying the accuracy of our dental revenue cycle management services. Operating as an independent contractor, you will assist in reviewing billing processes, insurance claims, clearinghouses, and documentation to ensure compliance with company standards and industry regulations. This role requires a keen attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and the ability to work independently while contributing to the overall quality of our services.





How the Quality Control Auditor fits in:



The Quality Control Auditor plays a vital role in maintaining the integrity and accuracy of our dental revenue cycle management processes. As an independent contractor, you will work closely with the Quality Control Manager and other tea
mbers to identify errors, ensure compliance, and support process improvements. Your contributions will help safeguard the quality of our services, leading to improved client satisfaction and operational efficiency.



In this role, you will:



Audit Support:



Assist in auditing various aspects of the dental revenue cycle management process, including insurance claims, payment postings, and outstanding claims.



Data Verification:



Verify the accuracy and consistency of data within billing systems, ensuring that all entries comply with company standards and industry regulations.



Compliance Monitoring:



Help monitor compliance with healthcare regulations, including HIPAA, and ensure that all billing practices adhere to legal and ethical standards.



Documentation and Reporting:



Maintain accurate records of audit findings and assist in preparing reports for the Quality Control Manager.



Collaboration and Communication:



Work closely with the Quality Control Manager and other tea
mbers to identify discrepancies and provide feedback for process improvements.



Continuous Learning:



Stay informed about industry trends and updates in dental revenue cycle management to ensure ongoing compliance and quality improvement.



Self-Management and Independence:



Manage your tasks effectively, demonstrating the ability to work independently while maintaining high standards of accuracy and quality.





Success Criteria:



Compliance Adherence:



Ensure 100% compliance with healthcare regulations and company policies in all tasks performed.



Timely Task Completion:



Auditors are expected to complete 3-5 audits per day, depending on complexity. 



Collaboration and Feedback:



Provide actionable feedback to tea
mbers, leading to process improvements and enhanced accuracy in billing operations.



Salary: $5.63 per hour



Requirements:



1+ year of experience in an administrative or support role with dental insurance.

Experience working as an independent contractor is preferred.

Familiarity with working with independent contractors on a global scale.

High School diploma or GED; some college coursework is a plus.

Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook, etc.), Google Workspace, and familiarity with practice management softwares (e.g., Dentrix, Eaglesoft, Open Dental, etc.).

Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple tasks independently.

Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal.

Ability to work independently and manage your own workload effectively.









Our Company Values:



Customer Success

Transparency

Reliability

Professional Excellence

Alignment
